papers.title: Mean Mode Screaming: Mean--Variance Split Residuals for 1000-Layer Diffusion Transformers
papers.abstract: Abstract Deep diffusion transformers face structural instability at extreme depths due to mean-dominated collapse triggered by mean mode screaming, which is mitigated through mean-variance split residuals that maintain stable training while preserving performance.  AI-generated summary Scaling Diffusion Transformers (DiTs) to hundreds of layers introduces a structural vulnerability: networks can enter a silent, mean-dominated collapse state that homogenizes token representations and suppresses centered variation. Through mechanistic auditing, we isolate the trigger event of this collapse as Mean Mode Screaming (MMS). MMS can occur even when training appears stable, with a mean-coherent backward shock on residual writers that opens deep residual branches and drives the network into a mean-dominated state. We show this behavior is driven by an exact decomposition of these gradients into mean-coherent and centered components, compounded by the structural suppression of attention-logit gradients through the null space of the Softmax Jacobian once values homogenize.   To address this, we propose Mean-Variance Split (MV-Split) Residuals, which combine a separately gained centered residual update with a leaky trunk-mean replacement. On a 400-layer single-stream DiT, MV-Split prevents the divergent collapse that crashes the un-stabilized baseline; it tracks close to the baseline's pre-crash trajectory while remaining substantially better than token-isotropic gating methods such as LayerScale across the full schedule. Finally, we present a 1000-layer DiT as a scale-validation run at boundary scales, establishing that the architecture remains stably trainable at extreme depth.

ru.title: Как стабилизировать обучение экстремально глубоких диффузионных трансформеров
ru.desc: В статье исследуется проблема структурной нестабильности глубоких диффузионных трансформеров, которые при масштабировании до сотен слоёв могут коллапсировать в состояние, где представления токенов становятся однородными. Авторы выявили механизм коллапса, называемый Mean Mode Screaming (MMS), при котором градиенты в остаточных связях становятся сосредоточены на среднем значении, подавляя вариативность в представлениях. Предложено решение в виде Mean-Variance Split (MV-Split) остаточных связей, которые раздельно обрабатывают центрированную компоненту и среднее значение с использованием отдельных коэффициентов масштабирования. Предложенный метод успешно позволяет обучать диффузионные трансформеры глубиной до 1000 слоёв, сохраняя стабильность обучения и качество модели.

en.title: Stabilizing Deep Diffusion Transformers with MV-Split Residuals
en.desc: This paper addresses the issue of structural instability in deep diffusion transformers (DiTs) when scaled to extreme depths, which can lead to a phenomenon called mean-dominated collapse. This collapse is triggered by an event known as Mean Mode Screaming (MMS), where the network's token representations become homogenized, reducing variation and performance. To combat this, the authors introduce Mean-Variance Split (MV-Split) Residuals, which effectively stabilize training by maintaining a balance between mean and centered updates. Their experiments demonstrate that MV-Split Residuals allow for stable training of DiTs with up to 1000 layers, outperforming traditional methods and preventing performance degradation.

zh.title: 均值-方差分裂残差：稳定深度扩散变换器的关键
zh.desc: 深度扩散变换器在极深层次上面临结构不稳定性，主要是由于均值主导的崩溃现象，这种现象由均值模式尖叫（MMS）引发。为了缓解这一问题，本文提出了均值-方差分裂残差（MV-Split），该方法通过结合中心残差更新和泄漏的主干均值替换，保持了训练的稳定性并提高了性能。我们通过机制审计，识别出崩溃的触发事件为均值模式尖叫，即使在训练看似稳定时也会发生。实验结果表明，MV-Split在400层的单流DiT上有效防止了崩溃，并在极深层次上保持了可训练性。

papers.title: MACE-Dance: Motion-Appearance Cascaded Experts for Music-Driven Dance Video Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract MACE-Dance is a music-driven dance video generation framework that combines cascaded Mixture-of-Experts with diffusion models and specialized training strategies to achieve high-quality visual appearance and realistic human motion.  AI-generated summary With the rise of online dance-video platforms and rapid advances in AI-generated content (AIGC), music-driven dance generation has emerged as a compelling research direction. Despite substantial progress in related domains such as music-driven 3D dance generation, pose-driven image animation, and audio-driven talking-head synthesis, existing methods cannot be directly adapted to this task. Moreover, the limited studies in this area still struggle to jointly achieve high-quality visual appearance and realistic human motion. Accordingly, we present MACE-Dance, a music-driven dance video generation framework with cascaded Mixture-of-Experts (MoE). The Motion Expert performs music-to-3D motion generation while enforcing kinematic plausibility and artistic expressiveness, whereas the Appearance Expert carries out motion- and reference-conditioned video synthesis, preserving visual identity with spatiotemporal coherence. Specifically, the Motion Expert adopts a diffusion model with a BiMamba-Transformer hybrid architecture and a Guidance-Free Training (GFT) strategy, achieving state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in 3D dance generation. The Appearance Expert employs a decoupled kinematic-aesthetic fine-tuning strategy, achieving state-of-the-art (SOTA) performance in pose-driven image animation. To better benchmark this task, we curate a large-scale and diverse dataset and design a motion-appearance evaluation protocol. Based on this protocol, MACE-Dance also achieves state-of-the-art performance. Code is available at https://github.com/AMAP-ML/MACE-Dance.

ru.title: Музыка танцует: генерация видео через специализированных экспертов
ru.desc: MACE-Dance — это фреймворк для генерации видео танцев под музыку, использующий архитектуру Mixture-of-Experts с двумя специализированными экспертами. Motion Expert генерирует реалистичные 3D движения танца из музыки, применяя диффузионную модель с гибридной архитектурой BiMamba-Transformer и стратегию Guidance-Free Training. Appearance Expert синтезирует видео высокого качества на основе движений и эталонного видео, сохраняя визуальную идентичность и пространственно-временную согласованность. Система достигает передовых результатов как в качестве движений, так и в визуальном оформлении благодаря специализированным стратегиям обучения для каждого эксперта.

en.title: MACE-Dance: Harmonizing Music and Dance with AI
en.desc: MACE-Dance is a framework designed to generate dance videos that are synchronized with music, utilizing advanced machine learning techniques. It employs a cascaded Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) approach, where the Motion Expert focuses on creating realistic 3D motions that align with the music, while the Appearance Expert ensures the visual quality of the generated videos. The framework incorporates a diffusion model and a hybrid architecture to enhance the quality of motion generation, achieving state-of-the-art results. Additionally, MACE-Dance introduces a new dataset and evaluation protocol to effectively measure the performance of music-driven dance generation.

zh.title: 音乐驱动的舞蹈视频生成新突破
zh.desc: MACE-Dance是一个基于音乐的舞蹈视频生成框架，结合了级联混合专家模型和扩散模型，采用专门的训练策略，以实现高质量的视觉效果和真实的人体运动。该框架的运动专家负责将音乐转换为3D运动，同时确保运动的合理性和艺术表现力，而外观专家则进行运动和参考条件的视频合成，保持视觉身份的一致性和时空连贯性。MACE-Dance在3D舞蹈生成和姿态驱动图像动画方面都达到了最先进的性能。为了更好地评估这一任务，我们创建了一个大规模多样化的数据集，并设计了运动-外观评估协议。

papers.title: Flow-OPD: On-Policy Distillation for Flow Matching Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Flow-OPD addresses limitations in Flow Matching text-to-image models through a two-stage alignment approach combining on-policy distillation and manifold anchor regularization, achieving significant improvements in generation quality and alignment metrics.  AI-generated summary Existing Flow Matching (FM) text-to-image models suffer from two critical bottlenecks under multi-task alignment: the reward sparsity induced by scalar-valued rewards, and the gradient interference arising from jointly optimizing heterogeneous objectives, which together give rise to a 'seesaw effect' of competing metrics and pervasive reward hacking. Inspired by the success of On-Policy Distillation (OPD) in the large language model community, we propose Flow-OPD, the first unified post-training framework that integrates on-policy distillation into Flow Matching models. Flow-OPD adopts a two-stage alignment strategy: it first cultivates domain-specialized teacher models via single-reward GRPO fine-tuning, allowing each expert to reach its performance ceiling in isolation; it then establishes a robust initial policy through a Flow-based Cold-Start scheme and seamlessly consolidates heterogeneous expertise into a single student via a three-step orchestration of on-policy sampling, task-routing labeling, and dense trajectory-level supervision. We further introduce Manifold Anchor Regularization (MAR), which leverages a task-agnostic teacher to provide full-data supervision that anchors generation to a high-quality manifold, effectively mitigating the aesthetic degradation commonly observed in purely RL-driven alignment. Built upon Stable Diffusion 3.5 Medium, Flow-OPD raises the GenEval score from 63 to 92 and the OCR accuracy from 59 to 94, yielding an overall improvement of roughly 10 points over vanilla GRPO, while preserving image fidelity and human-preference alignment and exhibiting an emergent 'teacher-surpassing' effect. These results establish Flow-OPD as a scalable alignment paradigm for building generalist text-to-image models.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong
University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Гармонизация противоречивых целей в генерации изображений через дистилляцию экспертов
ru.desc: Flow-OPD — это метод выравнивания моделей генерации изображений по текстам на основе Flow Matching через двухэтапный подход. На первом этапе обучаются специализированные учителя с помощью GRPO для каждой задачи отдельно, затем их знания консолидируются в один студентную модель через on-policy дистилляцию. Авторы вводят регуляризацию Manifold Anchor Regularization, которая якорирует генерацию к многообразию высокого качества, предотвращая деградацию эстетики при обучении с подкреплением. Метод существенно улучшает качество генерации: GenEval поднялась с 63 до 92, точность OCR с 59 до 94 по сравнению с базовым GRPO.

en.title: Enhancing Text-to-Image Models with Flow-OPD
en.desc: Flow-OPD is a novel framework designed to enhance text-to-image models by addressing key challenges in multi-task alignment. It employs a two-stage alignment strategy that first fine-tunes specialized teacher models and then consolidates their expertise into a single student model. The introduction of Manifold Anchor Regularization helps maintain high-quality image generation while reducing common issues seen in reinforcement learning-driven approaches. Overall, Flow-OPD significantly improves generation quality and alignment metrics, making it a promising solution for developing versatile text-to-image models.

zh.title: Flow-OPD：提升文本到图像生成的对齐与质量
zh.desc: Flow-OPD是一种新颖的文本到图像生成模型，旨在解决现有Flow Matching模型在多任务对齐中的瓶颈问题。它采用两阶段对齐策略，首先通过单奖励的GRPO微调培养领域专门的教师模型，然后通过基于流的冷启动方案建立稳健的初始策略。该方法引入了流形锚定正则化，利用任务无关的教师提供全数据监督，从而有效减轻了纯强化学习驱动对齐中常见的美学退化问题。Flow-OPD在生成质量和对齐指标上取得了显著提升，展示了其作为构建通用文本到图像模型的可扩展对齐范式。

papers.title: HyperEyes: Dual-Grained Efficiency-Aware Reinforcement Learning for Parallel Multimodal Search Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract HyperEyes is a parallel multimodal search agent that enables concurrent entity searches while optimizing inference efficiency through dual-grained reinforcement learning and a specialized benchmark for evaluating both accuracy and efficiency.  AI-generated summary Existing multimodal search agents process target entities sequentially, issuing one tool call per entity and accumulating redundant interaction rounds whenever a query decomposes into independent sub-retrievals. We argue that effective multimodal agents should search wider rather than longer: dispatching multiple grounded queries concurrently within a round. To this end, we present HyperEyes, a parallel multimodal search agent that fuses visual grounding and retrieval into a single atomic action, enabling concurrent search across multiple entities while treating inference efficiency as a first-class training objective. HyperEyes is trained in two stages. For cold-start supervision, we develop a Parallel-Amenable Data Synthesis Pipeline covering visual multi-entity and textual multi-constraint queries, curating efficiency-oriented trajectories via Progressive Rejection Sampling. Building on this, our central contribution, a Dual-Grained Efficiency-Aware Reinforcement Learning framework, operates at two levels. At the macro level, we propose TRACE (Tool-use Reference-Adaptive Cost Efficiency), a trajectory-level reward whose reference is monotonically tightened during training to suppress superfluous tool calls without restricting genuine multi-hop search. At the micro level, we adapt On-Policy Distillation to inject dense token-level corrective signals from an external teacher on failed rollouts, mitigating the credit-assignment deficiency of sparse outcome rewards. Since existing benchmarks evaluate accuracy as the sole metric, omitting inference cost, we introduce IMEB, a human-curated benchmark of 300 instances that jointly evaluates search capability and efficiency. Across six benchmarks, HyperEyes-30B surpasses the strongest comparable open-source agent by 9.9% in accuracy with 5.3x fewer tool-call rounds on average.

ru.title: Ищи шире, а не дольше: параллельный поиск с оптимизацией эффективности
ru.desc: HyperEyes — это параллельный мультимодальный агент поиска, который позволяет одновременно искать несколько сущностей в одном раунде вместо последовательной обработки. Агент тренируется в два этапа: сначала на синтезированных данных с эффективными траекториями, а затем с помощью двухуровневого обучения с подкреплением, которое оптимизирует как точность, так и эффективность вычислений. На макроуровне используется метрика TRACE для подавления лишних вызовов инструментов, а на микроуровне применяется дистилляция для передачи сигналов коррекции от учителя. Авторы также представляют новый бенчмарк IMEB для оценки как точности поиска, так и эффективности использования ресурсов.

en.title: HyperEyes: Efficient Concurrent Multimodal Search Agent
en.desc: HyperEyes is a novel multimodal search agent designed to perform concurrent searches for multiple entities, enhancing inference efficiency through a dual-grained reinforcement learning approach. Unlike traditional agents that process queries sequentially, HyperEyes allows for simultaneous grounded queries, reducing redundant interactions. It employs a two-stage training process, utilizing a specialized data synthesis pipeline and a unique reward system that balances accuracy and efficiency. The introduction of the IMEB benchmark enables a comprehensive evaluation of both search capability and inference cost, demonstrating significant improvements over existing agents.

zh.title: 并行多模态搜索的高效新方式
zh.desc: HyperEyes 是一种并行多模态搜索代理，能够同时进行多个实体的搜索，并通过双粒度强化学习优化推理效率。与现有的多模态搜索代理不同，HyperEyes 允许在一个回合内并发处理多个查询，从而减少冗余的交互轮次。该系统通过两阶段训练，首先使用并行友好的数据合成管道进行冷启动监督，然后采用双粒度效率感知强化学习框架来提升搜索效率。HyperEyes 在六个基准测试中表现优异，准确率比最强的开源代理高出 9.9%，同时工具调用轮次平均减少 5.3 倍。

papers.title: Listwise Policy Optimization: Group-based RLVR as Target-Projection on the LLM Response Simplex
papers.abstract: Abstract Group-based policy gradient methods in reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards share a common geometric structure, leading to the development of Listwise Policy Optimization that explicitly handles target projection through divergence minimization for improved training performance and stability.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards (RLVR) has become a standard approach for large language models (LLMs) post-training to incentivize reasoning capacity. Among existing recipes, group-based policy gradient is prevalent, which samples a group of responses per prompt and updates the policy via group-relative advantage signals. This work reveals that these optimization strategies share a common geometric structure: each implicitly defines a target distribution on the response simplex and projects toward it via first-order approximation. Building on this insight, we propose Listwise Policy Optimization (LPO) to explicitly conduct the target-projection, which demystifies the implicit target by restricting the proximal RL objective to the response simplex, and then projects the policy via exact divergence minimization. This framework provides (i) monotonic improvement on the listwise objective with bounded, zero-sum, and self-correcting projection gradients, and (ii) flexibility in divergence selection with distinct structural properties through the decoupled projection step. On diverse reasoning tasks and LLM backbones, LPO consistently improves training performance over typical policy gradient baselines under matched targets, while intrinsically preserving optimization stability and response diversity.

ru.title: Явная проекция целевого распределения для оптимизации групповой политики
ru.desc: Статья исследует групповые методы градиентной политики в обучении с подкреплением с проверяемыми наградами и обнаруживает их общую геометрическую структуру. Авторы предлагают Listwise Policy Optimization (LPO) — новый алгоритм, который явно проводит проекцию целевого распределения на симплекс ответов через минимизацию дивергенции. Метод обеспечивает монотонное улучшение целевой функции с гарантированно ограниченными градиентами проекции и позволяет гибко выбирать различные типы дивергенций. На различных задачах рассуждения и архитектурах больших языковых моделей LPO показывает стабильное улучшение качества обучения при сохранении разнообразия генерируемых ответов.

en.title: Enhancing Reinforcement Learning with Listwise Policy Optimization
en.desc: This paper introduces Listwise Policy Optimization (LPO), a new method in reinforcement learning that focuses on improving training performance and stability for large language models. It builds on the concept of group-based policy gradient methods, which use groups of responses to update policies based on relative advantages. The authors highlight a common geometric structure in these methods, which allows for a more explicit target projection through divergence minimization. LPO demonstrates consistent improvements in training outcomes while maintaining stability and diversity in responses across various reasoning tasks.

zh.title: 提升强化学习的稳定性与性能
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的强化学习方法，称为列表策略优化（LPO），旨在提高训练性能和稳定性。LPO通过最小化散度，明确处理目标投影，从而优化策略。研究表明，现有的基于组的策略梯度方法共享相同的几何结构，这为LPO的提出提供了理论基础。通过在多种推理任务中应用，LPO在训练性能上优于传统的策略梯度基线，同时保持了优化的稳定性和响应的多样性。

papers.title: LLMs Improving LLMs: Agentic Discovery for Test-Time Scaling
papers.abstract: Abstract AutoTTS automates test-time scaling strategy discovery by formulating it as controller synthesis over reasoning trajectories and probe signals, achieving improved accuracy-cost tradeoffs with minimal computational overhead.  AI-generated summary Test-time scaling (TTS) has become an effective approach for improving large language model performance by allocating additional computation during inference. However, existing TTS strategies are largely hand-crafted: researchers manually design reasoning patterns and tune heuristics by intuition, leaving much of the computation-allocation space unexplored. We propose an environment-driven framework, AutoTTS, that changes what researchers design: from individual TTS heuristics to environments where TTS strategies can be discovered automatically. The key to AutoTTS lies in environment construction: the discovery environment must make the control space tractable and provide cheap, frequent feedback for TTS search. As a concrete instantiation, we formulate width--depth TTS as controller synthesis over pre-collected reasoning trajectories and probe signals, where controllers decide when to branch, continue, probe, prune, or stop and can be evaluated cheaply without repeated LLM calls. We further introduce beta parameterization to make the search tractable and fine-grained execution trace feedback to improve discovery efficiency by helping the agent diagnose why a TTS program fails. Experiments on mathematical reasoning benchmarks show that the discovered strategies improve the overall accuracy--cost tradeoff over strong manually designed baselines. The discovered strategies generalize to held-out benchmarks and model scales, while the entire discovery costs only $39.9 and 160 minutes. Our data, and code will be open-source at https://github.com/zhengkid/AutoTTS.

ru.title: Автоматическое открытие оптимальных стратегий масштабирования для языковых моделей
ru.desc: AutoTTS предлагает автоматический подход к открытию стратегий масштабирования во время тестирования (TTS) путём формулирования задачи как синтеза контроллера над траекториями рассуждений и зондирующими сигналами. Вместо ручного проектирования эвристик исследователи создают окружение, в котором стратегии TTS могут быть обнаружены автоматически через поиск. Метод использует предсобранные траектории рассуждений и дешёвую обратную связь для эффективного поиска, включая параметризацию бета и детальные трассировки выполнения. Экспериментально показано, что обнаруженные стратегии улучшают компромисс между точностью и вычислительными затратами, обобщаются на новые задачи и масштабы моделей с минимальными затратами на поиск.

en.title: Automating Test-Time Scaling for Enhanced Model Performance
en.desc: AutoTTS is a framework that automates the discovery of test-time scaling (TTS) strategies for large language models, enhancing their performance during inference. Instead of relying on manually crafted heuristics, AutoTTS utilizes an environment-driven approach to explore the computation-allocation space more effectively. It formulates the TTS problem as controller synthesis, allowing for efficient decision-making on when to branch or prune during reasoning. The results demonstrate that the strategies discovered by AutoTTS achieve better accuracy-cost tradeoffs compared to traditional methods, with minimal computational overhead.

zh.title: 自动化测试时间缩放策略发现
zh.desc: AutoTTS 是一个自动化的测试时间缩放策略发现框架，通过将其表述为控制器合成，利用推理轨迹和探测信号来实现更好的准确性与成本的权衡，同时保持最低的计算开销。传统的测试时间缩放策略主要依赖于人工设计，研究人员手动调整推理模式和启发式方法，导致计算分配空间未被充分探索。AutoTTS 通过构建环境，使得研究人员可以自动发现 TTS 策略，而不是单独设计每个启发式方法。实验结果表明，AutoTTS 发现的策略在数学推理基准测试中显著提高了准确性与成本的权衡，并且在不同的基准和模型规模上具有良好的泛化能力。

papers.title: HumanNet: Scaling Human-centric Video Learning to One Million Hours
papers.abstract: Abstract HumanNet presents a large-scale human-centric video dataset with rich annotations for embodied intelligence, demonstrating that egocentric human video can effectively replace robot data for training vision-language-action models.  AI-generated summary Progress in embodied intelligence increasingly depends on scalable data infrastructure. While vision and language have scaled with internet corpora, learning physical interaction remains constrained by the lack of large, diverse, and richly annotated human activity data. We present HumanNet, a one-million-hour human-centric video corpus that captures how humans interact with the physical world at scale. HumanNet spans both first-person and third-person perspectives and covers fine-grained activities, human-object interactions, tool use, and long-horizon behaviors across diverse real-world environments. Beyond raw video, the dataset provides interaction-centric annotations, including captions, motion descriptions, and hand and body-related signals, enabling motion-aware and interaction-aware learning. Beyond scale, HumanNet introduces a systematic data curation paradigm for embodied learning, where human-centric filtering, temporal structuring, viewpoint diversity, and annotation enrichment are treated as first-class design principles. This design transforms unstructured internet video into a scalable substrate for representation learning, activity understanding, motion generation, and human-to-robot transfer. We conduct a first-step validation on the value of this design through controlled vision-language-action ablation: under a fixed set of validation data, continued training from the Qwen VLM model with 1000 hours of egocentric video drawn from HumanNet surpasses the continued training with 100 hours of real-robot data from Magic Cobot, indicating that egocentric human video could be a scalable and cost-effective substitute for robot data. By building this project, we aim to explore the opportunity to scale embodied foundation models using human-centric videos, rather than relying solely on robot-specific data.

ru.title: Человеческие видео как замена робототехническим данным для обучения воплощённого интеллекта
ru.desc: HumanNet представляет набор данных из одного миллиона часов видео с человеческой активностью для обучения моделей воплощённого интеллекта. Датасет содержит богатые аннотации с описаниями действий, движений, взаимодействий с объектами и сигналами рук и тела как в эгоцентрической, так и в третьей перспективе. Авторы показали, что видео человеческих действий может эффективно заменить данные роботов при обучении видео-языково-действийных моделей. Это предлагает масштабируемый и экономически эффективный подход к развитию foundation-моделей для embodied AI без необходимости в дорогостоящих робототехнических данных.

en.title: Empowering AI with Human-Centric Video Data
en.desc: HumanNet is a large-scale dataset designed for training models in embodied intelligence using human-centric videos. It includes one million hours of video that captures human interactions with the environment, providing rich annotations for activities, interactions, and motions. The dataset allows for effective learning of vision-language-action models by using egocentric video as a substitute for traditional robot data. By implementing a systematic curation approach, HumanNet enhances the quality and diversity of the data, making it a valuable resource for advancing embodied AI research.

zh.title: 人类视频数据助力智能模型训练
zh.desc: HumanNet是一个大规模的人类中心视频数据集，专注于体现智能的研究。该数据集包含丰富的注释，展示了第一人称和第三人称视角下人类与物理世界的互动。通过提供动作描述、手部和身体信号等交互中心的注释，HumanNet支持运动感知和交互感知的学习。研究表明，使用HumanNet中的人类视频数据可以有效替代机器人数据，推动体现智能模型的训练。

papers.title: Anisotropic Modality Align
papers.abstract: Abstract Research addresses the modality gap in multimodal models by proposing an anisotropic geometric correction framework that enables effective unpaired modality alignment through structured representation transformation.  AI-generated summary Training multimodal large language models has long been limited by the scarcity of high-quality paired multimodal data. Recent studies show that the shared representation space of pretrained multimodal contrastive models can serve as a bridge, enabling models to perform multimodal training with unimodal data. However, the key premise of this paradigm remains insufficiently understood: can representations from different modalities be reliably interchanged? The core obstacle lies in the persistent Modality Gap in the shared space. In this work, we revisit the geometric nature of the modality gap. We find that modality representations already share compatible dominant semantic geometry. What truly hinders modality interchangeability is not a simple global shift, but an anisotropic residual structure concentrated along a small number of dominant directions. Based on this finding, we further propose the principle of anisotropic modality gap alignment: effective modality alignment should align with the target-modality distribution while preserving the semantic structure of the source modality. Guided by this principle, we propose an anisotropic geometric correction framework, AnisoAlign, for unpaired modality alignment. This framework leverages the internal geometric prior of the target modality and performs bounded correction on source-modality representations, thereby constructing substitute representations in the target modality. Experiments confirm its benefits in both geometric diagnostics and text-only MLLM training. Overall, this work recasts the modality gap from an empirical observation into a correctable, structured geometric phenomenon and provides a new representation alignment perspective for training multimodal models with unimodal data.

ru.title: Геометрическая коррекция разрыва между модальностями для обучения без парных данных
ru.desc: В работе исследуется проблема модальностного разрыва в мультимодальных моделях путём анализа геометрических свойств представлений разных модальностей. Авторы обнаружили, что основной障碍 заключается не в глобальном сдвиге, а в анизотропной остаточной структуре вдоль доминирующих направлений. На основе этого открытия предложена рамка AnisoAlign для выравнивания непарных модальностей через структурированное геометрическое преобразование представлений. Метод позволяет эффективно обучать мультимодальные LLM на однозначных данных, сохраняя семантическую структуру исходной модальности.

en.title: Bridging the Modality Gap with Geometric Correction
en.desc: This paper addresses the challenge of aligning different types of data, known as modalities, in multimodal models. It introduces a framework called AnisoAlign, which corrects the geometric differences between modalities to enable effective training with unpaired data. The authors discover that the main issue is not just a simple shift in representation but a complex structure that needs to be adjusted. By focusing on the geometric properties of the data, this work provides a new way to align representations, improving the training of multimodal models using only single-type data.

zh.title: 解决多模态模型中的模态差距
zh.desc: 本研究提出了一种各向异性几何校正框架，以解决多模态模型中的模态差距问题。该框架通过结构化表示转换，实现了有效的无配对模态对齐。研究发现，不同模态的表示在语义几何上是兼容的，但模态之间的可互换性受到各向异性残差结构的阻碍。通过这一发现，提出了各向异性模态差距对齐的原则，为使用单模态数据训练多模态模型提供了新的视角。

papers.title: Beyond Retrieval: A Multitask Benchmark and Model for Code Search
papers.abstract: Abstract A new code search benchmark called CoREB is introduced that addresses limitations of existing datasets by providing contamination-limited, multitask evaluation across text-to-code, code-to-text, and code-to-code retrieval tasks with fine-tuned reranking capabilities.  AI-generated summary Code search has usually been evaluated as first-stage retrieval, even though production systems rely on broader pipelines with reranking and developer-style queries. Existing benchmarks also suffer from data contamination, label noise, and degenerate binary relevance. In this paper, we introduce CoREB, a contamination-limited, multitask code retrieval and reranking benchmark, together with a fine-tuned code reranker, that goes beyond retrieval to cover the full code search pipeline. CoREB is built from counterfactually rewritten LiveCodeBench problems in five programming languages and delivered as timed releases with graded relevance judgments. We benchmark eleven embedding models and five rerankers across three tasks: text-to-code, code-to-text, and code-to-code. Our experiments reveal that: \circone code-specialised embeddings dominate code-to-code retrieval ({sim}2{times} over general encoders), yet no single model wins all three tasks; \circtwo short keyword queries, the format closest to real developer search, collapse every model to near-zero nDCG@10; \circthree off-the-shelf rerankers are task-asymmetric, with a 12-point swing on code-to-code and no baseline net-positive across all tasks; \circfour our fine-tuned CoREB-Reranker is the first to achieve consistent gains across all three tasks. The data and model are released.

ru.title: Комплексный бенчмарк для оценки полного pipeline поиска кода с переранжированием
ru.desc: В статье представлен новый бенчмарк CoREB для оценки поиска кода, который решает проблемы существующих наборов данных, включая загрязнение данных и шумные разметки. Бенчмарк охватывает полный pipeline поиска кода с переранжированием и включает три типа задач: поиск кода по текстовому описанию, поиск текста по коду и поиск кода по коду. Авторы протестировали одиннадцать моделей векторных представлений и пять переранжировщиков, обнаружив, что специализированные модели превосходят универсальные на определённых задачах, но ни одна не выигрывает во всех случаях. Они предложили собственный обученный переранжировщик CoREB-Reranker, который впервые показывает стабильные улучшения на всех трёх задачах.

en.title: Revolutionizing Code Search with CoREB: A Multitask Benchmark
en.desc: The paper introduces CoREB, a new benchmark for code search that improves upon existing datasets by minimizing contamination and enabling multitask evaluation. It focuses on three retrieval tasks: text-to-code, code-to-text, and code-to-code, while incorporating a fine-tuned reranking system. The study benchmarks various embedding models and rerankers, revealing that specialized embeddings perform better in code-to-code retrieval, but no single model excels in all tasks. Additionally, the paper highlights the challenges of using short keyword queries and presents the CoREB-Reranker as a solution that consistently improves performance across all tasks.

zh.title: CoREB：全面提升代码搜索的基准
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种新的代码搜索基准CoREB，旨在解决现有数据集的局限性。CoREB提供了污染限制的多任务评估，涵盖文本到代码、代码到文本和代码到代码的检索任务，并具备精细调整的重排序能力。通过对五种编程语言的LiveCodeBench问题进行反事实重写，CoREB构建了一个全面的代码搜索管道。实验结果表明，专门针对代码的嵌入模型在代码到代码的检索中表现优异，而我们的CoREB-Reranker在所有三个任务中都实现了一致的性能提升。

papers.title: TextLDM: Language Modeling with Continuous Latent Diffusion
papers.abstract: Abstract TextLDM adapts visual latent diffusion transformers to language modeling by mapping discrete tokens to continuous latents and using representation alignment for improved text generation quality.  AI-generated summary Diffusion Transformers (DiT) trained with flow matching in a VAE latent space have unified visual generation across images and videos. A natural next step toward a single architecture for both generation (visual synthesis) and understanding (text generation) is to apply this framework to language modeling. We propose TextLDM, which transfers the visual latent diffusion recipe to text generation with minimal architectural modification. A Transformer-based VAE maps discrete tokens to continuous latents, enhanced by Representation Alignment (REPA) with a frozen pretrained language model to produce representations effective for conditional denoising. A standard DiT then performs flow matching in this latent space, identical in architecture to its visual counterpart. The central challenge we address is obtaining high-quality continuous text representations: we find that reconstruction fidelity alone is insufficient, and that aligning latent features with a pretrained language model via REPA is critical for downstream generation quality. Trained from scratch on OpenWebText2, TextLDM substantially outperforms prior diffusion language models and matches GPT-2 under the same settings. Our results establish that the visual DiT recipe transfers effectively to language, taking a concrete step toward unified diffusion architectures for multimodal generation and understanding.

ru.title: Единая архитектура диффузии для генерации текста и изображений
ru.desc: TextLDM адаптирует архитектуру диффузионных трансформеров из компьютерного зрения для генерации текста, преобразуя дискретные токены в непрерывные представления в пространстве скрытых состояний VAE. Ключевое улучшение — метод выравнивания представлений (REPA), который согласует скрытые признаки с предварительно обученной языковой моделью для получения качественных представлений. Модель использует flow matching в латентном пространстве с архитектурой, идентичной визуальным диффузионным трансформерам, что позволяет унифицировать подход к генерации и пониманию. При обучении с нуля на корпусе OpenWebText2 модель превосходит предыдущие диффузионные модели языка и показывает результаты, сравнимые с GPT-2.

en.title: Unifying Visual and Text Generation with TextLDM
en.desc: This paper introduces TextLDM, a novel approach that adapts visual latent diffusion transformers for language modeling. By mapping discrete tokens to continuous latents and employing Representation Alignment (REPA) with a pretrained language model, TextLDM enhances text generation quality. The architecture utilizes a Transformer-based Variational Autoencoder (VAE) and a standard Diffusion Transformer (DiT) to perform flow matching in the latent space. The results demonstrate that TextLDM significantly improves upon previous diffusion language models and achieves performance comparable to GPT-2, indicating the effectiveness of applying visual diffusion techniques to text generation.

zh.title: 统一视觉与语言生成的扩散模型
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的文本生成模型TextLDM，它将视觉潜在扩散变换器的思想应用于语言建模。通过将离散标记映射到连续潜在空间，并使用表示对齐技术，TextLDM能够提高文本生成的质量。研究表明，仅靠重建保真度不足以获得高质量的文本表示，必须通过与预训练语言模型的对齐来提升生成效果。经过在OpenWebText2上的训练，TextLDM在性能上显著超越了之前的扩散语言模型，并在相同设置下与GPT-2相匹配。

papers.title: UniPrefill: Universal Long-Context Prefill Acceleration via Block-wise Dynamic Sparsification
papers.abstract: Abstract UniPrefill is a prefill acceleration framework that works across various model architectures and integrates seamlessly with vLLM to improve long-context inference efficiency.  AI-generated summary As large language models (LLMs) continue to advance rapidly, they are becoming increasingly capable while simultaneously demanding ever-longer context lengths. To improve the inference efficiency of long-context processing, several novel low-complexity hybrid architectures have recently been proposed, effectively alleviating the computational burden of long-context inference. However, existing research on long-context prefill acceleration remains predominantly focused on sparse attention mechanisms, which achieve their maximum speedup only on full-attention models. When transferred to emerging architectures--such as linear/full attention hybrids or sliding window/full attention hybrids--these prefill acceleration approaches suffer significant performance degradation. Furthermore, such methods are generally incompatible with continuous batching, making them difficult to integrate into modern inference engines such as vLLM. To this end, we propose UniPrefill, a prefill acceleration framework applicable to virtually any model architecture, which directly accelerates the model's computation at the token level. We further implement UniPrefill as a continuous batching operator and extend vLLM's scheduling strategy to natively support prefill-decode co-processing and tensor parallel for UniPrefill, enabling its seamless integration into vLLM. UniPrefill achieves up to 2.1x speedup in Time-To-First-Token (TTFT), with the acceleration becoming increasingly pronounced as the number of concurrent requests grows.

ru.title: Универсальное ускорение префилла для любых архитектур LLM
ru.desc: UniPrefill — это фреймворк для ускорения фазы префилл-обработки, который работает со множеством архитектур моделей и интегрируется с vLLM. Подход решает проблему неэффективности существующих методов ускорения на гибридных архитектурах (линейное внимание + полное внимание, скользящее окно + полное внимание), которые страдают от деградации производительности при переносе между моделями. Метод ускоряет вычисления на уровне токенов и реализован как оператор с непрерывной батчеризацией, позволяя одновременно обрабатывать фазы префилл и декодирования. Фреймворк достигает ускорения до 2.1x по метрике Time-To-First-Token, причём эффект ускорения растёт с увеличением числа одновременных запросов.

en.title: Accelerating Long-Context Inference with UniPrefill
en.desc: UniPrefill is a framework designed to speed up the prefill process in various model architectures, enhancing the efficiency of long-context inference. It addresses the limitations of existing methods that primarily focus on sparse attention mechanisms, which do not perform well with newer hybrid architectures. By operating at the token level, UniPrefill allows for continuous batching and integrates smoothly with the vLLM inference engine. This results in significant improvements in Time-To-First-Token (TTFT), achieving up to 2.1 times faster processing as the number of requests increases.

zh.title: UniPrefill：提升长上下文推理效率的加速框架
zh.desc: UniPrefill 是一个预填充加速框架，能够在多种模型架构中工作，并与 vLLM 无缝集成，以提高长上下文推理的效率。随着大型语言模型（LLMs）的快速发展，它们对上下文长度的需求也在不断增加。UniPrefill 通过在令牌级别直接加速模型计算，解决了现有方法在新兴架构中性能下降的问题。该框架实现了连续批处理操作，并扩展了 vLLM 的调度策略，使其能够支持预填充-解码协同处理，最终实现了高达 2.1 倍的首次令牌时间加速。

papers.title: DecodingTrust-Agent Platform (DTap): A Controllable and Interactive Red-Teaming Platform for AI Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract A comprehensive platform and autonomous agent framework for evaluating and enhancing AI agent security through controlled red-teaming across multiple real-world domains and simulation environments.  AI-generated summary AI agents are increasingly deployed across diverse domains to automate complex workflows through long-horizon and high-stakes action executions. Due to their high capability and flexibility, such agents raise significant security and safety concerns. A growing number of real-world incidents have shown that adversaries can easily manipulate agents into performing harmful actions, such as leaking API keys, deleting user data, or initiating unauthorized transactions. Evaluating agent security is inherently challenging, as agents operate in dynamic, untrusted environments involving external tools, heterogeneous data sources, and frequent user interactions. However, realistic, controllable, and reproducible environments for large-scale risk assessment remain largely underexplored. To address this gap, we introduce the DecodingTrust-Agent Platform (DTap), the first controllable and interactive red-teaming platform for AI agents, spanning 14 real-world domains and over 50 simulation environments that replicate widely used systems such as Google Workspace, Paypal, and Slack. To scale the risk assessment of agents in DTap, we further propose DTap-Red, the first autonomous red-teaming agent that systematically explores diverse injection vectors (e.g., prompt, tool, skill, environment, combinations) and autonomously discovers effective attack strategies tailored to varying malicious goals. Using DTap-Red, we curate DTap-Bench, a large-scale red-teaming dataset comprising high-quality instances across domains, each paired with a verifiable judge to automatically validate attack outcomes. Through DTap, we conduct large-scale evaluations of popular AI agents built on various backbone models, spanning security policies, risk categories, and attack strategies, revealing systematic vulnerability patterns and providing valuable insights for developing secure next-generation agents.

ru.title: Автоматическое красное тестирование для безопасности AI агентов
ru.desc: В статье представлена платформа DecodingTrust-Agent (DTap) для оценки безопасности AI агентов через контролируемое красное тестирование на 14 реальных доменах и 50+ симуляциях популярных систем. Авторы предлагают DTap-Red — автономный агент красного тестирования, который систематически исследует различные векторы атак (промпт-инъекции, манипуляции инструментами, окружением) и обнаруживает эффективные стратегии взлома. На основе платформы создан DTap-Bench — крупномасштабный датасет с высококачественными примерами атак и верифицируемыми судьями для автоматической валидации результатов. Проведённая оценка популярных AI агентов выявила системные уязвимости и предоставила ценные рекомендации для разработки безопасных агентов следующего поколения.

en.title: Enhancing AI Agent Security with DTap: A New Frontier in Red-Teaming
en.desc: This paper presents the DecodingTrust-Agent Platform (DTap), a novel framework designed to enhance the security of AI agents through controlled red-teaming across various real-world scenarios. It addresses the challenges of evaluating AI agent security in dynamic environments by providing a comprehensive platform that includes over 50 simulation environments. The DTap-Red autonomous agent is introduced to systematically explore different attack vectors and discover effective strategies for evaluating agent vulnerabilities. The research culminates in the creation of DTap-Bench, a dataset that aids in the large-scale assessment of AI agents, revealing critical insights into their security weaknesses.

zh.title: 提升AI代理安全性的创新平台
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一个名为DecodingTrust-Agent Platform (DTap) 的平台，旨在评估和增强人工智能代理的安全性。该平台支持在14个真实世界领域和50多个模拟环境中进行控制性红队测试，以识别代理的安全漏洞。我们还提出了DTap-Red，这是第一个自主红队代理，能够系统地探索多种攻击向量并发现有效的攻击策略。通过DTap，我们能够对流行的人工智能代理进行大规模评估，揭示系统性脆弱性模式，并为开发安全的下一代代理提供有价值的见解。

papers.title: AEM: Adaptive Entropy Modulation for Multi-Turn Agentic Reinforcement Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel supervision-free credit assignment method for reinforcement learning in language model agents that adapts entropy dynamics at the response level to improve exploration-exploitation trade-offs and task performance.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning (RL) has substantially improved the ability of large language model (LLM) agents to interact with environments and solve multi-turn tasks. However, effective agentic RL remains challenging: sparse outcome-only rewards provide limited guidance for assigning credit to individual steps within long interaction trajectories. Existing approaches often introduce dense intermediate supervision, such as process reward models or auxiliary self-supervised signals, which increases supervision and tuning complexity and may limit generalization across tasks and domains. We present AEM, a supervision-free credit assignment method that adaptively modulates entropy dynamics during RL training to improve the exploration-exploitation trade-off. Since in agentic RL the environment is typically affected by a complete response, rather than an individual token, our analysis lifts entropy dynamics from the token level to the response level, aligning uncertainty estimation with the effective action granularity of LLM agents and reducing sensitivity to token-level sampling noise. We further show that entropy drift under natural-gradient updates is governed by the interaction between the sampled-response advantage and its relative surprisal. Motivated by this result, AEM derives a practical response-level uncertainty proxy and uses it to rescale advantages, leveraging the evolving balance between positive and negative samples to naturally transition from exploration to exploitation. Extensive experiments on ALFWorld, WebShop, and SWE-bench-Verified with models ranging from 1.5B to 32B demonstrate that AEM consistently improves strong RL baselines, including a +1.4\% gain when integrated into a state-of-the-art software-engineering RL training framework.

ru.title: Адаптивное управление неопределённостью для эффективного кредита-ассайнмента в агентных системах
ru.desc: В работе представлен метод AEM для назначения кредитов в обучении с подкреплением агентов на основе языковых моделей без дополнительной супервизии. Основная идея заключается в адаптивной модуляции динамики энтропии на уровне полного ответа (а не отдельного токена), что лучше соответствует гранулярности действий LLM-агентов и улучшает баланс между исследованием и эксплуатацией. Метод использует прокси неопределённости на уровне ответа для масштабирования преимуществ, естественным образом переходя от разведочного к эксплуатационному поведению. Экспериментальные результаты на бенчмарках ALFWorld, WebShop и SWE-bench демонстрируют консистентное улучшение производительности по сравнению с сильными базовыми методами обучения с подкреплением.

en.title: Revolutionizing Reinforcement Learning with Supervision-Free Credit Assignment
en.desc: This paper introduces AEM, a new method for credit assignment in reinforcement learning (RL) that does not require additional supervision. It focuses on improving the balance between exploration and exploitation by adjusting entropy dynamics at the response level, rather than at the individual token level. This approach helps language model agents better understand the impact of their complete responses on the environment, leading to more effective learning. The results show that AEM enhances performance across various tasks and models, demonstrating its potential to simplify RL training while improving outcomes.

zh.title: 无监督信用分配，提升强化学习表现
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新颖的无监督信用分配方法AEM，旨在改善语言模型代理的强化学习（RL）性能。该方法通过在响应级别调整熵动态，优化探索与利用的平衡，从而提高任务表现。传统的RL方法依赖稀疏奖励，难以有效指导长交互过程中的每一步，而AEM则避免了对中间监督的依赖，简化了训练过程。实验结果表明，AEM在多个基准测试中显著提升了RL基线的表现，证明了其有效性。

papers.title: Rethinking State Tracking in Recurrent Models Through Error Control Dynamics
papers.abstract: Abstract Affine recurrent networks cannot correct errors in state tracking once state representations are preserved, leading to finite horizon solutions governed by accumulated error rather than robust tracking.  AI-generated summary The theory of state tracking in recurrent architectures has predominantly focused on expressive capacity: whether a fixed architecture can theoretically realize a set of symbolic transition rules. We argue that equally important is error control, the dynamics governing hidden-state drift along the directions that distinguish symbolic states. We prove that affine recurrent networks, a class of models encompassing State-Space Models and Linear Attention, cannot correct errors along state-separating subspaces once they preserve state representations. Consequently, practical affine trackers do not learn robust state tracking; rather, they learn finite horizon solutions governed by accumulated state-relevant error. We characterize the mechanics of this failure, showing that tracking remains readable only while the accumulating within-class spread remains small relative to the initial between-class separation. We demonstrate empirically on group state-tracking tasks that this breakdown is predictable: tracking collapses when the distinguishability ratio crosses the readability threshold of the trained decoder. Across trained models, the point of this crossing predicts the horizon at which downstream accuracy fails. These results establish that robust state tracking is determined not only by an architecture's theoretical expressivity but crucially by its error control.

ru.title: Контроль ошибок важнее выразительности в архитектурах отслеживания состояния
ru.desc: Статья исследует проблему отслеживания состояния в рекуррентных нейронных сетях, в частности в аффинных архитектурах, таких как State-Space Models и Linear Attention. Авторы доказывают, что эти модели не могут исправлять ошибки в представлении скрытого состояния, что приводит к накоплению ошибок со временем. Они показывают, что практические характеристики таких сетей ограничены не только теоретической выразительностью архитектуры, но и её способностью контролировать ошибки в процессе отслеживания. Исследование демонстрирует, что точность отслеживания состояния падает предсказуемо, когда накопленная ошибка становится соизмерима с расстоянием между различными состояниями.

en.title: Error Control is Key for Robust State Tracking in Recurrent Networks
en.desc: This paper discusses the limitations of affine recurrent networks in maintaining accurate state tracking over time. It highlights that while these networks can represent symbolic transitions, they struggle with error correction once state representations are established. The authors demonstrate that these models tend to produce solutions that are only valid for a limited time, as they accumulate errors that affect tracking performance. They provide empirical evidence showing that the ability to track states reliably diminishes when the error between classes becomes too large compared to the initial separation, indicating the importance of error control in model design.

zh.title: 稳健状态跟踪需重视错误控制
zh.desc: 本文探讨了仿射递归网络在状态跟踪中的局限性，特别是在状态表示被保留的情况下，无法纠正状态跟踪中的错误。我们指出，错误控制同样重要，它影响着隐藏状态沿着区分符号状态的方向的漂移。研究表明，仿射递归网络无法在状态分离的子空间中纠正错误，因此它们学习到的不是稳健的状态跟踪，而是受累积错误影响的有限视野解决方案。实验证明，当可区分性比率超过训练解码器的可读性阈值时，跟踪性能会崩溃，这一现象是可预测的。

papers.title: MatryoshkaLoRA: Learning Accurate Hierarchical Low-Rank Representations for LLM Fine-Tuning
papers.abstract: Abstract MatryoshkaLoRA introduces a hierarchical low-rank adaptation framework that dynamically adjusts rank selection through a diagonal matrix insertion, improving accuracy-performance trade-offs over existing methods.  AI-generated summary With the rise in scale for deep learning models to billions of parameters, the computational cost of fine-tuning remains a significant barrier to deployment. While Low-Rank Adaptation (LoRA) has become the standard for parameter-efficient fine-tuning, the need to set a predefined, static rank r requires exhaustive grid searches to balance efficiency and performance. Existing rank-adaptive solutions such as DyLoRA mitigate this by sampling ranks during the training from a predefined distribution. However, they often yield sub-optimal results at higher ranks due to lack of consistent gradient signals across the full hierarchy of ranks, thus making these methods data-inefficient. In this paper, we propose MatryoshkaLoRA, a general, Matryoshka-inspired training framework for LoRA that learns accurate hierarchical low-rank representations by inserting a fixed, carefully crafted diagonal matrix P between the existing LoRA adapters to scale their sub-ranks accordingly. By introducing this simple modification, our general framework recovers LoRA and DyLoRA only by changing P and ensures all sub-ranks embed the available gradient information efficiently. Our MatryoshkaLoRA supports dynamic rank selection with minimal degradation in accuracy. We further propose Area Under the Rank Accuracy Curve (AURAC), a metric that consistently evaluates the performance of hierarchical low-rank adapters. Our results demonstrate that MatryoshkaLoRA learns more accurate hierarchical low-rank representations than prior rank-adaptive approaches and achieves superior accuracy-performance trade-offs across ranks on the evaluated datasets. Our code is available at https://github.com/IST-DASLab/MatryoshkaLoRA.

ru.title: Иерархическая адаптация с динамическим выбором ранга для эффективного дообучения больших моделей
ru.desc: MatryoshkaLoRA представляет иерархическую структуру низкоранговой адаптации, которая динамически выбирает ранг матрицы через вставку диагональной матрицы между адаптерами LoRA. Метод решает проблему неэффективности существующих адаптивных подходов, которые страдают от несогласованных градиентов при работе с полной иерархией рангов. Предложенный фреймворк обеспечивает эффективное использование информации из градиентов на всех уровнях, позволяя выбирать ранг во время инференса с минимальной потерей точности. Авторы также введили метрику AURAC для объективной оценки производительности иерархических низкоранговых адаптеров.

en.title: Dynamic Rank Selection for Efficient Fine-Tuning
en.desc: MatryoshkaLoRA is a new framework designed to improve the fine-tuning of large deep learning models by using a hierarchical low-rank adaptation method. It dynamically adjusts the rank of model parameters through the insertion of a diagonal matrix, which helps to optimize the balance between accuracy and performance. Unlike previous methods that require fixed ranks, MatryoshkaLoRA allows for flexible rank selection, leading to better utilization of gradient information across different ranks. The framework also introduces a new evaluation metric, Area Under the Rank Accuracy Curve (AURAC), to assess the effectiveness of hierarchical low-rank adapters more reliably.

zh.title: 动态秩选择，提升低秩适应的准确性与性能
zh.desc: MatryoshkaLoRA提出了一种分层低秩适应框架，通过插入对角矩阵动态调整秩选择，从而改善了现有方法的准确性与性能之间的权衡。随着深度学习模型参数规模的增加，微调的计算成本成为部署的主要障碍。虽然低秩适应（LoRA）已成为参数高效微调的标准，但预定义的静态秩r需要进行大量的网格搜索以平衡效率和性能。MatryoshkaLoRA通过在现有LoRA适配器之间插入固定的对角矩阵P，学习准确的分层低秩表示，支持动态秩选择，确保所有子秩有效嵌入可用的梯度信息。

papers.title: 4DThinker: Thinking with 4D Imagery for Dynamic Spatial Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract 4DThinker enables vision-language models to perform dynamic spatial reasoning through 4D latent mental imagery, using scalable data generation and novel fine-tuning methods that outperform existing approaches.  AI-generated summary Dynamic spatial reasoning from monocular video is essential for bridging visual intelligence and the physical world, yet remains challenging for vision-language models (VLMs). Prior approaches either verbalize spatial-temporal reasoning entirely as text, which is inherently verbose and imprecise for complex dynamics, or rely on external geometric modules that increase inference complexity without fostering intrinsic model capability. In this paper, we present 4DThinker, the first framework that enables VLMs to "think with 4D" through dynamic latent mental imagery, i.e., internally simulating how scenes evolve within the continuous hidden space. Specifically, we first introduce a scalable, annotation-free data generation pipeline that synthesizes 4D reasoning data from raw videos. We then propose Dynamic-Imagery Fine-Tuning (DIFT), which jointly supervises textual tokens and 4D latents to ground the model in dynamic visual semantics. Building on this, 4D Reinforcement Learning (4DRL) further tackles complex reasoning tasks via outcome-based rewards, restricting policy gradients to text tokens to ensure stable optimization. Extensive experiments across multiple dynamic spatial reasoning benchmarks demonstrate that 4DThinker consistently outperforms strong baselines and offers a new perspective toward 4D reasoning in VLMs. Our code is available at https://github.com/zhangquanchen/4DThinker.
affiliations: The Chinese University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Обучение моделей думать в четырёхмерном пространстве
ru.desc: 4DThinker — это первая система, которая позволяет видео-языковым моделям выполнять динамическое пространственное рассуждение через внутреннее моделирование эволюции сцен в скрытом пространстве. Авторы разработали масштабируемый конвейер генерации данных без разметки, который синтезирует обучающие примеры из видео. Они предложили метод Dynamic-Imagery Fine-Tuning, который совместно обучает текстовые токены и четырёхмерные представления, а также 4D Reinforcement Learning для решения сложных задач рассуждения с использованием наград на основе результатов. Система показывает значительное улучшение по сравнению с существующими подходами на множестве бенчмарков пространственного рассуждения.

en.title: Empowering Vision-Language Models with 4D Dynamic Reasoning
en.desc: The paper introduces 4DThinker, a novel framework that enhances vision-language models (VLMs) by enabling them to perform dynamic spatial reasoning using 4D latent mental imagery. It addresses the limitations of previous methods that either rely on verbose text descriptions or complex external geometric modules. The framework includes a scalable data generation pipeline that creates 4D reasoning data from raw videos and employs Dynamic-Imagery Fine-Tuning (DIFT) to improve model grounding in dynamic visual semantics. Additionally, it incorporates 4D Reinforcement Learning (4DRL) to optimize reasoning tasks through outcome-based rewards, demonstrating superior performance on various benchmarks.

zh.title: 4DThinker：动态空间推理的新视角
zh.desc: 4DThinker是一个新框架，使视觉-语言模型能够通过4D潜在心理图像进行动态空间推理。它采用可扩展的数据生成和新颖的微调方法，超越了现有的技术。该方法通过从原始视频合成4D推理数据，避免了繁琐的文本描述和外部几何模块的复杂性。实验结果表明，4DThinker在多个动态空间推理基准测试中表现优异，展示了视觉-语言模型在4D推理方面的新视角。

papers.title: MISA: Mixture of Indexer Sparse Attention for Long-Context LLM Inference
papers.abstract: Abstract MISA replaces the dense token-wise indexing in sparse attention with a routed mixture-of-experts approach that reduces computational cost while maintaining performance and handling long contexts effectively.  AI-generated summary DeepSeek Sparse Attention (DSA) sets the state of the art for fine-grained inference-time sparse attention by introducing a learned token-wise indexer that scores every prefix token and selects the most relevant ones for the main attention. To remain expressive, the indexer uses many query heads (for example, 64 on DeepSeek-V3.2) that share the same selected token set; this multi-head design is precisely what makes the indexer the dominant cost on long contexts. We propose MISA (Mixture of Indexer Sparse Attention), a drop-in replacement for the DSA indexer that treats its indexer heads as a pool of mixture-of-experts. A lightweight router uses cheap block-level statistics to pick a query-dependent subset of only a few active heads, and only those heads run the heavy token-level scoring. This preserves the diversity of the original indexer pool while reducing the per-query cost from scoring every prefix token with every head to scoring it with only a handful of routed heads, plus a negligible router term computed on a small set of pooled keys. We further introduce a hierarchical variant of MISA that uses the routed pass to keep an enlarged candidate set and then re-ranks it with the original DSA indexer to recover the final selected tokens almost exactly. With only eight active heads and no additional training, MISA matches the dense DSA indexer on LongBench across DeepSeek-V3.2 and GLM-5 while running with eight and four times fewer indexer heads respectively, and outperforms HISA on average. It also preserves fully green Needle-in-a-Haystack heatmaps up to a 128K-token context and recovers more than 92% of the tokens selected by the DSA indexer per layer. Our TileLang kernel delivers roughly a 3.82 times speedup over DSA's original indexer kernel on a single NVIDIA H200 GPU.

ru.title: Экспертная маршрутизация для эффективного разреженного внимания
ru.desc: В работе предложен метод MISA, который заменяет плотное индексирование токенов в разреженном внимании на подход смеси экспертов. Это значительно снижает вычислительные затраты при обработке длинных контекстов, сохраняя качество работы модели. Легкий маршрутизатор выбирает небольшое подмножество головок внимания для каждого запроса, используя дешевые блочные статистики, что избегает необходимости вычисления оценок для каждого токена со всеми головками. Иерархический вариант MISA дополнительно переранжирует候выбранные токены исходным методом, достигая ускорения в 3.82 раза без потери качества на задачах с длинными контекстами.

en.title: Efficient Sparse Attention with Mixture-of-Experts
en.desc: The paper introduces MISA, a new method for sparse attention in machine learning that improves efficiency by using a mixture-of-experts approach. Instead of scoring every token with all query heads, MISA routes a small subset of active heads to reduce computational costs while maintaining performance. This method allows for effective handling of long contexts and retains the diversity of the original indexer. MISA achieves comparable results to the existing Dense Sparse Attention indexer while significantly speeding up processing times on large datasets.

zh.title: MISA：高效的稀疏注意力新选择
zh.desc: MISA（混合索引稀疏注意力）通过路由混合专家的方法，替代了稠密的逐标记索引，从而降低了计算成本，同时保持了性能并有效处理长上下文。该方法引入了一种轻量级的路由器，利用低成本的块级统计信息选择少量活跃的查询头，减少了每个查询的计算负担。MISA在DeepSeek-V3.2和GLM-5上与稠密的DSA索引器表现相当，同时使用的索引头数量分别减少了八倍和四倍。此外，MISA在处理128K标记上下文时，能够保留原始DSA索引器选择的92%以上的标记。

papers.title: A^2RD: Agentic Autoregressive Diffusion for Long Video Consistency
papers.abstract: Abstract A$^2$RD, an Agentic Auto-Regressive Diffusion architecture, addresses long video synthesis challenges through a closed-loop process with memory tracking, adaptive generation, and hierarchical self-improvement mechanisms.  AI-generated summary Synthesizing consistent and coherent long video remains a fundamental challenge. Existing methods suffer from semantic drift and narrative collapse over long horizons. We present A^2RD, an Agentic Auto-Regressive Diffusion architecture that decouples creative synthesis from consistency enforcement. A^2RD formulates long video synthesis as a closed-loop process that synthesizes and self-improves video segment-by-segment through a Retrieve--Synthesize--Refine--Update cycle. It comprises three core components: (i) Multimodal Video Memory that tracks video progression across modalities; (ii) Adaptive Segment Generation that switches among generation modes for natural progression and visual consistency; and (iii) Hierarchical Test-Time Self-Improvement that self-improves each segment at frame and video levels to prevent error propagation. We further introduce LVBench-C, a challenging benchmark with non-linear entity and environment transitions to stress-test long-horizon consistency. Across public and LVBench-C benchmarks spanning one- to ten-minute videos, A^2RD outperforms state-of-the-art baselines by up to 30% in consistency and 20% in narrative coherence. Human evaluations corroborate these gains while also highlighting notable improvements in motion and transition smoothness.

ru.title: Самоулучшающаяся диффузия для синтеза длинных видео без дрейфа смысла
ru.desc: A²RD — это агентивная архитектура диффузионной модели для синтеза длинных видеоматериалов, которая использует замкнутый цикл Retrieve–Synthesize–Refine–Update для поэтапного генерирования и улучшения видеосегментов. Система включает три ключевых компонента: мультимодальную память видео для отслеживания прогрессии контента, адаптивную генерацию сегментов с выбором оптимального режима синтеза, и иерархическое самоулучшение на уровне кадров и видео для предотвращения накопления ошибок. Авторы представили новый бенчмарк LVBench-C с нелинейными переходами сущностей и окружения для проверки устойчивости моделей к долгосрочным зависимостям. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют превосходство A²RD над существующими методами на 30% по метрикам консистентности и 20% по связности повествования при синтезе видео длительностью от одной до десяти минут.

en.title: Revolutionizing Long Video Synthesis with A^2RD
en.desc: The paper introduces A^2RD, an innovative architecture designed for generating long videos while maintaining consistency and coherence. It employs a closed-loop process that allows for memory tracking and adaptive generation, addressing common issues like semantic drift. A^2RD operates through a Retrieve-Synthesize-Refine-Update cycle, enhancing video quality segment by segment. The architecture includes a Multimodal Video Memory, Adaptive Segment Generation, and Hierarchical Test-Time Self-Improvement, leading to significant improvements in video synthesis performance compared to existing methods.

zh.title: 长视频合成的新突破：A$^2$RD架构
zh.desc: A$^2$RD是一种代理自回归扩散架构，旨在解决长视频合成中的挑战。它通过闭环过程进行视频合成，结合记忆跟踪、适应性生成和分层自我改进机制。该方法将长视频合成视为一个循环过程，通过检索、合成、精炼和更新的周期逐段生成和自我改进视频。实验结果表明，A$^2$RD在一致性和叙事连贯性方面显著优于现有方法。

papers.title: UniSD: Towards a Unified Self-Distillation Framework for Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Self-distillation framework UniSD systematically addresses challenges in autoregressive language model adaptation through integrated mechanisms for supervision reliability, representation alignment, and training stability.  AI-generated summary Self-distillation (SD) offers a promising path for adapting large language models (LLMs) without relying on stronger external teachers. However, SD in autoregressive LLMs remains challenging because self-generated trajectories are free-form, correctness is task-dependent, and plausible rationales can still provide unstable or unreliable supervision. Existing methods mainly examine isolated design choices, leaving their effectiveness, roles, and interactions unclear. In this paper, we propose UniSD, a unified framework to systematically study self-distillation. UniSD integrates complementary mechanisms that address supervision reliability, representation alignment, and training stability, including multi-teacher agreement, EMA teacher stabilization, token-level contrastive learning, feature matching, and divergence clipping. Across six benchmarks and six models from three model families, UniSD reveals when self-distillation improves over static imitation, which components drive the gains, and how these components interact across tasks. Guided by these insights, we construct UniSDfull, an integrated pipeline that combines complementary components and achieves the strongest overall performance, improving over the base model by +5.4 points and the strongest baseline by +2.8 points. Extensive evaluation highlights self-distillation as a practical and steerable approach for efficient LLM adaptation without stronger external teachers.

ru.title: Самодистилляция языковых моделей через интегрированные механизмы стабильности и надежности
ru.desc: В работе предложена унифицированная структура UniSD для самодистилляции авторегрессивных языковых моделей, которая решает проблему надежности обучающего сигнала от самогенерируемых траекторий. Фреймворк интегрирует несколько дополняющих механизмов: согласованность нескольких учителей, стабилизацию через экспоненциальное скользящее среднее, контрастивное обучение на уровне токенов и ограничение расхождения. Авторы проводят систематическое исследование взаимодействия компонентов на шести бенчмарках и показывают, когда самодистилляция превосходит статическую имитацию. Достигнутая интегрированная версия UniSDfull показывает улучшение на 5.4 пункта над базовой моделью, демонстрируя эффективность подхода для адаптации больших языковых моделей без более мощного учителя.

en.title: UniSD: Enhancing Language Model Adaptation through Self-Distillation
en.desc: The paper presents UniSD, a self-distillation framework designed to enhance the adaptation of autoregressive language models. It addresses key challenges such as supervision reliability, representation alignment, and training stability by integrating various mechanisms like multi-teacher agreement and token-level contrastive learning. The study evaluates the effectiveness of these components across multiple benchmarks and models, revealing how they interact and contribute to performance improvements. Ultimately, UniSDfull demonstrates significant gains in model performance, showcasing self-distillation as a viable method for adapting large language models without the need for stronger external teachers.

zh.title: 自我蒸馏：高效适应大语言模型的新路径
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种自我蒸馏框架UniSD，旨在解决自回归语言模型适应中的挑战。UniSD通过整合多种机制，提高了监督的可靠性、表示的一致性和训练的稳定性。研究表明，自我蒸馏在某些任务上优于静态模仿，并揭示了各个组件之间的相互作用。最终，UniSDfull作为一个综合管道，显著提升了模型性能，证明了自我蒸馏在大语言模型适应中的有效性。

papers.title: Normalizing Trajectory Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Normalizing Trajectory Models introduce a novel approach to diffusion-based generation by modeling each reverse step as an expressive conditional normalizing flow with exact likelihood training, enabling high-quality sample generation in few steps while maintaining likelihood framework.  AI-generated summary Diffusion-based models decompose sampling into many small Gaussian denoising steps -- an assumption that breaks down when generation is compressed to a few coarse transitions. Existing few-step methods address this through distillation, consistency training, or adversarial objectives, but sacrifice the likelihood framework in the process. We introduce Normalizing Trajectory Models (NTM), which models each reverse step as an expressive conditional normalizing flow with exact likelihood training. Architecturally, NTM combines shallow invertible blocks within each step with a deep parallel predictor across the trajectory, forming an end-to-end network trainable from scratch or initializable from pretrained flow-matching models. Its exact trajectory likelihood further enables self-distillation: a lightweight denoiser trained on the model's own score produces high-quality samples in four steps. On text-to-image benchmarks, NTM matches or outperforms strong image generation baselines in just four sampling steps while uniquely retaining exact likelihood over the generative trajectory.

ru.title: Правдоподобие в каждом шаге: диффузия без компромиссов
ru.desc: В работе предложены Normalizing Trajectory Models — новый подход к диффузионным моделям, который представляет каждый обратный шаг как выразительный условный нормализующий поток с точным обучением по правдоподобию. Это позволяет генерировать высококачественные образцы всего за несколько шагов вместо множества шагов в традиционных диффузионных моделях. В отличие от существующих методов сжатия (дистилляция, consistency training), NTM сохраняет возможность точного расчета правдоподобия траектории генерации. Архитектура модели объединяет неглубокие обратимые блоки в каждом шаге с глубоким параллельным предсказателем, позволяя достичь выдающихся результатов в синтезе изображений за четыре шага.

en.title: Efficient and Likely: Revolutionizing Sample Generation with NTM
en.desc: Normalizing Trajectory Models (NTM) present a new way to generate samples using diffusion-based methods by treating each reverse step as a conditional normalizing flow. This approach allows for high-quality sample generation in just a few steps while still adhering to a likelihood framework. Unlike previous methods that compromise likelihood for efficiency, NTM maintains exact likelihood training, enabling effective self-distillation for improved sample quality. The architecture combines shallow invertible blocks with a deep predictor, achieving competitive results in text-to-image generation tasks.

zh.title: 正常化轨迹模型：高效生成的创新之路
zh.desc: 正常化轨迹模型（NTM）提出了一种新颖的扩散生成方法，通过将每个反向步骤建模为具有精确似然训练的条件正常化流，从而实现高质量样本的生成。与传统的扩散模型不同，NTM能够在仅需少量步骤的情况下保持生成的准确性。该模型结合了浅层可逆块和深层并行预测器，形成一个可从头开始训练的端到端网络。通过自蒸馏技术，NTM在文本到图像的基准测试中，在仅四个采样步骤内生成高质量图像，且保持了生成轨迹的精确似然性。

papers.title: Q-RAG: Long Context Multi-step Retrieval via Value-based Embedder Training
papers.abstract: Abstract Q-RAG enables efficient multi-step retrieval for large language models through reinforcement learning fine-tuning of embedder models, achieving state-of-the-art performance on long-context benchmarks.  AI-generated summary Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) methods enhance LLM performance by efficiently filtering relevant context for LLMs, reducing hallucinations and inference cost. However, most existing RAG methods focus on single-step retrieval, which is often insufficient for answering complex questions that require multi-step search. Recently, multi-step retrieval approaches have emerged, typically involving the fine-tuning of small LLMs to perform multi-step retrieval. This type of fine-tuning is highly resource-intensive and does not enable the use of larger LLMs. In this work, we propose Q-RAG, a novel approach that fine-tunes the Embedder model for multi-step retrieval using reinforcement learning (RL). Q-RAG offers a competitive, resource-efficient alternative to existing multi-step retrieval methods for open-domain question answering and achieves state-of-the-art results on the popular long-context benchmarks BabiLong and RULER for contexts up to 10M tokens. Code is available at https://github.com/griver/Q-RAG
affiliations: Higher School of Economics, Moscow, Russia
London Institute for Mathematical Sciences, London, UK
Research Center of the Artificial Intelligence Institute, Innopolis University, Innopolis, Russia

ru.title: Многошаговый поиск для LLM через обучение с подкреплением эмбеддингов
ru.desc: Q-RAG — это инновационный подход к пополнению контекста для больших языковых моделей, который использует обучение с подкреплением для оптимизации модели эмбеддингов на многошаговый поиск. В отличие от существующих методов, которые требуют дорогостоящей дообучения малых LLM, Q-RAG позволяет эффективнее искать релевантную информацию через несколько этапов поиска. Метод достигает передовых результатов на бенчмарках с экстремально длинными контекстами (до 10 миллионов токенов), одновременно снижая вычислительные затраты. Данный подход значительно улучшает качество ответов на сложные вопросы, требующие многошагового поиска в больших документах.

en.title: Q-RAG: Efficient Multi-Step Retrieval for Enhanced LLM Performance
en.desc: Q-RAG is a new method that improves how large language models (LLMs) retrieve information by using reinforcement learning to fine-tune embedder models. This approach allows for efficient multi-step retrieval, which is crucial for answering complex questions that require looking through large amounts of data. Unlike traditional methods that often rely on single-step retrieval, Q-RAG can handle longer contexts, making it more effective in reducing errors and costs associated with generating responses. The method has shown to achieve top performance on long-context benchmarks, demonstrating its potential in open-domain question answering.

zh.title: Q-RAG：高效的多步检索新方法
zh.desc: Q-RAG是一种新颖的方法，通过强化学习对嵌入模型进行微调，实现高效的多步检索，特别适用于大型语言模型。该方法在长上下文基准测试中表现出色，能够有效减少幻觉现象和推理成本。与传统的单步检索方法相比，Q-RAG能够处理更复杂的问题，提供更准确的答案。它为开放域问答提供了一种资源高效的替代方案，适用于处理高达10M标记的上下文。

papers.title: SCOPE: Structured Decomposition and Conditional Skill Orchestration for Complex Image Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract SCOPE is a specification-guided framework that maintains semantic commitments throughout text-to-image generation to improve complex visual intent fulfillment.  AI-generated summary While text-to-image models have made strong progress in visual fidelity, faithfully realizing complex visual intents remains challenging because many requirements must be tracked across grounding, generation, and verification. We refer to these requirements as semantic commitments and formalize their lifecycle discontinuity as the Conceptual Rift, where commitments may be locally resolved or checked but fail to remain identifiable as the same operational units throughout the generation lifecycle. To address this, we propose SCOPE, a specification-guided skill orchestration framework that maintains semantic commitments in an evolving structured specification and conditionally invokes retrieval, reasoning, and repair skills around unresolved or violated commitments. To evaluate commitment-level intent realization, we introduce Gen-Arena, a human-annotated benchmark with entity- and constraint-level specifications, together with Entity-Gated Intent Pass Rate (EGIP), a strict entity-first pass criterion. SCOPE substantially outperforms all evaluated baselines on Gen-Arena, achieving 0.60 EGIP, and further achieves strong results on WISE-V (0.907) and MindBench (0.61), demonstrating the effectiveness of persistent commitment tracking for complex image generation.
affiliations: University of Science and Technology of China

ru.title: Сохранение семантических обязательств для точной генерации сложных изображений
ru.desc: SCOPE — это фреймворк, управляемый спецификациями, который отслеживает семантические обязательства на протяжении всего процесса генерации изображений по текстовому описанию. Авторы формализуют проблему «Концептуального разрыва», когда требования теряют идентичность по мере прохождения через этапы генерации и верификации. Предложенный метод использует оркестрацию навыков (поиск, рассуждение и исправление), чтобы поддерживать структурированную спецификацию и разрешать нарушенные обязательства. Эффективность подхода подтверждается на новом бенчмарке Gen-Arena с метрикой EGIP, а также на существующих датасетах WISE-V и MindBench.

en.title: SCOPE: Bridging the Conceptual Rift in Text-to-Image Generation
en.desc: The paper introduces SCOPE, a framework designed to enhance text-to-image generation by maintaining semantic commitments throughout the process. It identifies the challenge of the Conceptual Rift, where visual intents can become disconnected during generation. SCOPE addresses this by orchestrating skills for retrieval, reasoning, and repair to ensure that these commitments are consistently tracked.
