The framework is evaluated using the Gen-Arena benchmark, showing significant improvements in fulfilling complex visual intents compared to existing methods.

zh.title: SCOPE：保持语义承诺的图像生成框架
zh.desc: SCOPE是一个规范引导的框架，旨在文本到图像生成过程中保持语义承诺，以提高复杂视觉意图的实现。尽管文本到图像模型在视觉真实感方面取得了显著进展，但忠实实现复杂视觉意图仍然具有挑战性，因为需要在生成的各个阶段跟踪许多要求。我们将这些要求称为语义承诺，并将其生命周期的不连续性形式化为概念裂缝，承诺可能在局部得到解决或检查，但在整个生成生命周期中未能保持可识别性。为了解决这个问题，我们提出了SCOPE框架，通过条件调用检索、推理和修复技能，围绕未解决或违反的承诺，维护一个不断演变的结构化规范。

papers.title: What Matters for Diffusion-Friendly Latent Manifold? Prior-Aligned Autoencoders for Latent Diffusion
papers.abstract: Abstract Research investigates latent manifold properties for diffusion models and proposes a Prior-Aligned AutoEncoder that explicitly optimizes latent space structure for improved generative modeling.  AI-generated summary Tokenizers are a crucial component of latent diffusion models, as they define the latent space in which diffusion models operate. However, existing tokenizers are primarily designed to improve reconstruction fidelity or inherit pretrained representations, leaving unclear what kind of latent space is truly friendly for generative modeling. In this paper, we study this question from the perspective of latent manifold organization. By constructing controlled tokenizer variants, we identify three key properties of a diffusion-friendly latent manifold: coherent spatial structure, local manifold continuity, and global manifold semantics. We find that these properties are more consistent with downstream generation quality than reconstruction fidelity. Motivated by this finding, we propose the Prior-Aligned AutoEncoder (PAE), which explicitly shapes the latent manifold instead of leaving diffusion-friendly manifold to emerge indirectly from reconstruction or inheritance. Specifically, PAE leverages refined priors derived from VFMs and perturbation-based regularization to turn spatial structure, local continuity, and global semantics into explicit training objectives. On ImageNet 256x256, PAE improves both training efficiency and generation quality over existing tokenizers, reaching performance comparable to RAE with up to 13x faster convergence under the same training setup and achieving a new state-of-the-art gFID of 1.03. These results highlight the importance of organizing the latent manifold for latent diffusion models.
affiliations: Shenzhen Institutes of Advanced Technology, Chinese Academy of Sciences

ru.title: Явная организация латентного многообразия для улучшения диффузионного моделирования
ru.desc: В работе исследуются свойства скрытого многообразия в диффузионных моделях и предлагается Prior-Aligned AutoEncoder, который явно оптимизирует структуру латентного пространства. Авторы выявили три ключевых свойства благоприятного для генерации многообразия: связная пространственная структура, локальная непрерывность и глобальная семантика. Было обнаружено, что эти свойства лучше коррелируют с качеством генерации, чем с точностью реконструкции. Предложенный метод PAE использует уточненные приоры из VFMs и регуляризацию на основе возмущений, чтобы явно учитывать эти свойства в процессе обучения.

en.title: Optimizing Latent Space for Better Generative Models
en.desc: This paper explores how to improve generative modeling in diffusion models by focusing on the structure of the latent space. It identifies three important properties for a diffusion-friendly latent manifold: coherent spatial structure, local manifold continuity, and global manifold semantics. The authors introduce the Prior-Aligned AutoEncoder (PAE), which explicitly optimizes these properties during training rather than relying on indirect methods. Their results show that PAE significantly enhances both training efficiency and generation quality, achieving state-of-the-art performance on ImageNet.

zh.title: 优化潜在流形，提升生成质量
zh.desc: 本研究探讨了扩散模型的潜在流形特性，并提出了一种优先对齐自编码器（Prior-Aligned AutoEncoder），该模型明确优化潜在空间结构以改善生成建模。现有的标记器主要关注重建精度或继承预训练表示，未能清晰定义适合生成建模的潜在空间。我们通过构建受控的标记器变体，识别出扩散友好的潜在流形的三个关键特性：一致的空间结构、局部流形连续性和全局流形语义。实验结果表明，这些特性与下游生成质量的相关性高于重建精度，因此我们提出的PAE模型在训练效率和生成质量上均优于现有标记器。

papers.title: Scaling Continual Learning to 300+ Tasks with Bi-Level Routing Mixture-of-Experts
papers.abstract: Abstract A novel continual learning framework called CaRE with a bi-level routing mixture-of-experts mechanism is proposed for class-incremental learning, demonstrating superior performance on very long task sequences exceeding 300 tasks.  AI-generated summary Continual learning, especially class-incremental learning (CIL), on the basis of a pre-trained model (PTM) has garnered substantial research interest in recent years. However, how to effectively learn both discriminative and comprehensive feature representations while maintaining stability and plasticity over very long task sequences remains an open problem. We propose CaRE, a scalable {C}ontinual Le{a}rner with efficient Bi-Level {R}outing Mixture-of-{E}xperts (BR-MoE). The core idea of BR-MoE is a bi-level routing mechanism: a router selection stage that dynamically activates relevant task-specific routers, followed by an expert routing phase that dynamically activates and aggregates experts, aiming to inject discriminative and comprehensive representations into every intermediate network layer. On the other hand, we introduce a challenging dataset, OmniBenchmark-1K, for CIL performance evaluation on very long task sequences with hundreds of tasks. Extensive experiments show that CaRE demonstrates leading performance across a variety of datasets and task settings, including commonly used CIL datasets with classical CIL settings (e.g., 5-20 tasks). To the best of our knowledge, CaRE is the first continual learner that scales to very long task sequences (ranging from 100 to over 300 non-overlapping tasks), while outperforming all baselines by a large margin on such task sequences. We hope that this work will inspire further research into continual learning over extremely long task sequences. Code and dataset are publicly released at https://github.com/LMMMEng/CaRE.
affiliations: Center for Embodied Artificial Intelligence and Computer Vision, Shenzhen Loop Area Institute
School of Computing and Data Science, The University of Hong Kong

ru.title: Непрерывное обучение на бесконечных потоках данных через интеллектуальную маршрутизацию экспертов
ru.desc: В статье предлагается CaRE — фреймворк для непрерывного обучения с двухуровневым механизмом маршрутизации смеси экспертов, который эффективно решает задачу пошагового обучения новым классам. Основная идея заключается в динамической активации специализированных маршрутизаторов и экспертов на каждом слое сети для получения дискриминативных и полных представлений признаков. Авторы вводят новый бенчмарк OmniBenchmark-1K для оценки алгоритмов непрерывного обучения на очень длинных последовательностях задач (более 300). Экспериментальные результаты показывают, что CaRE превосходит все базовые методы и впервые масштабируется до таких длинных последовательностей задач, сохраняя баланс между стабильностью и пластичностью модели.

en.title: CaRE: Mastering Continual Learning with Dynamic Expert Routing
en.desc: The paper introduces CaRE, a new framework for continual learning that excels in class-incremental learning (CIL) with very long task sequences. It employs a bi-level routing mixture-of-experts (BR-MoE) mechanism, which allows for dynamic selection and aggregation of task-specific routers and experts. This approach helps the model learn both detailed and broad feature representations while maintaining stability and adaptability. The authors also present a new dataset, OmniBenchmark-1K, to evaluate CIL performance across hundreds of tasks, demonstrating that CaRE significantly outperforms existing methods.

zh.title: CaRE：应对超长任务序列的持续学习新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的持续学习框架CaRE，采用双层路由混合专家机制，专门用于类增量学习。CaRE能够在超过300个任务的长序列上表现出色，解决了在保持稳定性和可塑性同时有效学习特征表示的问题。其核心思想是通过动态激活任务特定的路由器和专家，注入区分性和全面性的表示到每个中间网络层。我们还引入了一个新的数据集OmniBenchmark-1K，用于评估在长任务序列上的持续学习性能。

papers.title: STARFlow2: Bridging Language Models and Normalizing Flows for Unified Multimodal Generation
papers.abstract: Abstract Autoregressive normalizing flows based on Transformer architecture enable unified multimodal generation by aligning text and image processing through shared causal masking and KV-cache mechanisms.  AI-generated summary Deep generative models have advanced rapidly across text and vision, motivating unified multimodal systems that can understand, reason over, and generate interleaved text-image sequences. Most existing approaches combine autoregressive language modeling with diffusion-based image generators, inheriting a structural mismatch between causal text generation and iterative visual denoising. We observe that autoregressive normalizing flows are autoregressive Transformers--sharing the same causal mask, KV-cache mechanism, and left-to-right structure as LLMs--making them the most natural paradigm for true unified multimodal generation. We present STARFlow2, built on the Pretzel architecture that vertically interleaves a pretrained VLM stream with a TarFlow stream via residual skip connections, both operating under the same causal mask. Combined with a deep-shallow flow design and a unified FAE latent space, STARFlow2 enables cache-friendly interleaved generation where both text and visual outputs directly enter the KV-cache without re-encoding. Experiments demonstrate strong performance across image generation and multimodal understanding benchmarks, validating autoregressive flows as a viable foundation for unified multimodal modeling.

ru.title: Единая мультимодальная генерация через авторегрессивные нормализующие потоки
ru.desc: В статье предлагается STARFlow2 — система для единого мультимодального поколения текста и изображений на основе авторегрессивных нормализующих потоков и архитектуры Transformer. Авторы показывают, что авторегрессивные нормализующие потоки имеют ту же структуру, что и LLM, с причинной маской и механизмом KV-кэша, что позволяет избежать несогласованности между генерацией текста и итеративной денойзацией изображений. Модель использует архитектуру Pretzel, которая переплетает поток предварительно обученной VLM с потоком TarFlow через остаточные пропускные соединения, оба работают под единой причинной маской. Экспериментальные результаты демонстрируют сильную производительность на задачах генерации изображений и понимания мультимодальных данных.

en.title: Unified Multimodal Generation with STARFlow2
en.desc: This paper introduces STARFlow2, a model that uses autoregressive normalizing flows based on Transformer architecture for generating both text and images together. It aligns text and image processing by utilizing shared causal masking and KV-cache mechanisms, allowing for seamless multimodal generation. Unlike previous methods that combine different models, STARFlow2 integrates a pretrained vision-language model with a flow-based text generator, ensuring a consistent structure. The results show that this approach performs well in generating images and understanding multimodal content, proving that autoregressive flows can effectively unify text and image generation.

zh.title: 统一多模态生成的自回归归一化流
zh.desc: 这篇论文介绍了一种基于Transformer架构的自回归归一化流模型，旨在实现文本和图像的统一生成。通过共享因果掩码和KV缓存机制，该模型能够同时处理文本和图像数据。研究者提出了STARFlow2模型，它结合了预训练的视觉语言模型和自回归流，能够高效地生成交错的文本和图像序列。实验结果表明，该模型在图像生成和多模态理解任务上表现出色，验证了自回归流在统一多模态建模中的有效性。

papers.title: What if AI systems weren't chatbots?
papers.abstract: Abstract  The rapid convergence of artificial intelligence (AI) toward conversational chatbot interfaces marks a critical moment for the industry. This paper argues that the chatbot paradigm is not a neutral interface choice, but a dominant sociotechnical configuration whose widespread adoption reshapes social, economic, legal, and environmental systems. We examine how treating AI primarily as conversational assistants has extensive structural downsides. We show how chatbot-based systems often fail to adequately meet user needs, particularly in complex or high-stakes contexts, while projecting confidence and authority. We further analyze how the normalization of chatbot-mediated interaction alters patterns of work, learning, and decision-making, contributing to deskilling, homogenization of knowledge, and shifting expectations of expertise. Finally, we examine broader societal effects, including labor displacement, concentration of economic power, and increased environmental costs driven by sustained investment in large-scale chatbot infrastructures. While acknowledging legitimate benefits, we argue that the current trajectory of AI development reflects specific value choices that prioritize conversational generality over domain specificity, accountability, and long-term social sustainability. We conclude by outlining alternative directions for AI development and governance that move beyond one-size-fits-all chatbots, emphasizing pluralistic system design, task-specific tools, and institutional safeguards to mitigate social and economic harm.

ru.title: За пределами универсальных чат-ботов: переосмысление стратегии развития искусственного интеллекта
ru.desc: Статья критически анализирует доминирование чат-ботов в развитии искусственного интеллекта, показывая, что это не нейтральный выбор интерфейса, а социотехническая конфигурация с глубокими последствиями. Авторы демонстрируют, что системы на основе LLM часто неадекватно решают сложные задачи, создавая иллюзию компетентности, одновременно приводя к деградации профессиональных навыков и гомогенизации знаний. Работа выявляет широкие социальные и экономические негативные эффекты: вытеснение рабочей силы, концентрацию власти и значительные экологические издержки из-за вычислительных требований крупномасштабной инфраструктуры. Авторы предлагают альтернативный путь развития AI, ориентированный на специализированные инструменты, плюралистический дизайн систем и институциональные механизмы защиты от социального вреда.

en.title: Rethinking Chatbots: Beyond Simple Conversations for a Sustainable Future
en.desc: This paper discusses the rise of conversational chatbots in artificial intelligence and their significant impact on society. It argues that chatbots are not just simple tools but shape social and economic systems in ways that can be harmful. The authors highlight that these systems often fail to address complex user needs and can lead to negative outcomes like deskilling and labor displacement. They propose that AI development should focus on more specialized tools and governance to ensure social sustainability and accountability.

zh.title: 聊天机器人：机遇与挑战的双刃剑
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了人工智能（AI）向对话式聊天机器人界面的快速发展对社会的影响。作者认为，聊天机器人并不是中立的界面选择，而是一种主导的社会技术配置，其广泛应用改变了社会、经济、法律和环境系统。研究表明，聊天机器人系统在复杂或高风险的环境中往往无法满足用户需求，尽管它们表现出自信和权威。最后，论文提出了AI发展的替代方向，强调多元化系统设计和任务特定工具，以减少社会和经济的负面影响。

papers.title: IntentGrasp: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Intent Understanding
papers.abstract: Abstract IntentGrasp is a benchmark for evaluating large language models' intent understanding capability, demonstrating poor performance across 20 models and showing significant improvements with intentional fine-tuning.  AI-generated summary Accurately understanding the intent behind speech, conversation, and writing is crucial to the development of helpful Large Language Model (LLM) assistants. This paper introduces IntentGrasp, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating the intent understanding capability of LLMs. Derived from 49 high-quality, open-licensed corpora spanning 12 diverse domains, IntentGrasp is constructed through source datasets curation, intent label contextualization, and task format unification. IntentGrasp contains a large-scale training set of 262,759 instances and two evaluation sets: an All Set of 12,909 test cases and a more balanced and challenging Gem Set of 470 cases. Extensive evaluations on 20 LLMs across 7 families (including frontier models such as GPT-5.4, Gemini-3.1-Pro, and Claude-Opus-4.7) demonstrate unsatisfactory performance, with scores below 60% on All Set and below 25% on Gem set. Notably, 17 out of 20 tested models perform worse than a random-guess baseline (15.2%) on Gem Set, while the estimated human performance is ~81.1%, showing substantial room for improvement. To enhance such ability, this paper proposes Intentional Fine-Tuning (IFT), which fine-tunes the models on the training set in IntentGrasp, yielding significant gains of 30+ F1 points on All Set and 20+ points on Gem Set. Tellingly, the leave-one-domain-out (Lodo) experiments further demonstrate the strong cross-domain generalizability of IFT, verifying that it is a promising approach to substantially enhancing the intent understanding of LLMs. Overall, by benchmarking and boosting intent understanding ability, this study sheds light on a promising path towards more intentional, capable, and safe AI assistants for human benefits and social good.
affiliations: Department of Computer Science, University of British Columbia

ru.title: Обучение LLM правильно понимать истинные намерения пользователя
ru.desc: IntentGrasp — это комплексный бенчмарк для оценки способности больших языковых моделей понимать намерения в речи и текстах, созданный из 49 открытых корпусов 12 различных предметных областей. Оценка 20 современных LLM показала неудовлетворительные результаты: модели набирают менее 60% на основном наборе тестов и менее 25% на более сложном сбалансированном наборе. Для улучшения этих результатов авторы предложили метод Intentional Fine-Tuning (IFT), который дообучает модели на специализированном наборе данных IntentGrasp, достигая прироста более 30 пунктов F1-метрики. Эксперименты с исключением доменов показали сильную способность предложенного подхода к переносу на новые предметные области, делая IFT перспективным методом для развития более безопасных и компетентных AI-ассистентов.

en.title: Enhancing Intent Understanding in AI with IntentGrasp
en.desc: The paper introduces IntentGrasp, a benchmark designed to evaluate how well large language models (LLMs) understand intent in various forms of communication. It highlights that 20 tested models performed poorly, scoring below 60% on a comprehensive test set and below 25% on a more challenging subset. To improve performance, the authors propose Intentional Fine-Tuning (IFT), which significantly enhances intent understanding by fine-tuning models on the IntentGrasp training set. The results indicate that IFT can lead to substantial improvements, suggesting a pathway for developing more effective AI assistants.

zh.title: 提升大型语言模型的意图理解能力
zh.desc: IntentGrasp是一个用于评估大型语言模型意图理解能力的基准，显示出20个模型的表现不佳。该基准由49个高质量的开放许可语料库构成，涵盖12个不同领域，包含262,759个训练实例。通过意图标签的上下文化和任务格式的统一，IntentGrasp提供了全面的评估工具。为提高模型的意图理解能力，本文提出了意图微调（IFT），在训练集上进行微调，显著提升了模型的表现。

papers.title: SkCC: Portable and Secure Skill Compilation for Cross-Framework LLM Agents
papers.abstract: Abstract SkCC is a compilation framework that uses a strongly-typed intermediate representation to enable portable deployment of agent skills across different platforms while ensuring security and improving performance.  AI-generated summary LLM-Agents have evolved into autonomous systems for complex task execution, with the SKILL.md specification emerging as a de facto standard for encapsulating agent capabilities. However, a critical bottleneck remains: different agent frameworks exhibit starkly different sensitivities to prompt formatting, causing up to 40% performance variation, yet nearly all skills exist as a single, format-agnostic Markdown version. Manual per-platform rewriting creates an unsustainable maintenance burden, while prior audits have found that over one third of community skills contain security vulnerabilities. To address this, we present SkCC, a compilation framework that introduces classical compiler design into agent skill development. At its core, SkIR - a strongly-typed intermediate representation - decouples skill semantics from platform-specific formatting, enabling portable deployment across heterogeneous agent frameworks. Around this IR, a compile-time Analyzer enforces security constraints via Anti-Skill Injection before deployment. Through a four-phase pipeline, SkCC reduces adaptation complexity from O(m times n) to O(m + n). Experiments on SkillsBench demonstrate that compiled skills consistently outperform their original counterparts, improving pass rates from 21.1% to 33.3% on Claude Code and from 35.1% to 48.7% on Kimi CLI, while achieving sub-10ms compilation latency, a 94.8% proactive security trigger rate, and 10-46% runtime token savings across platforms.

ru.title: Компиляция навыков агентов для кроссплатформенной совместимости и безопасности
ru.desc: SkCC представляет собой фреймворк компиляции, который использует строго типизированное промежуточное представление (SkIR) для автоматического преобразования навыков агентов, позволяя их портативное развёртывание на различных платформах. Основная проблема заключается в том, что разные framework-и для LLM-агентов имеют существенные различия в чувствительности к форматированию подсказок, что приводит к вариациям производительности до 40%, тогда как навыки обычно существуют в одной универсальной версии. SkCC решает эту задачу, применяя классические подходы компилятора и сокращая сложность адаптации с O(m×n) до O(m+n), при этом проводя анализ безопасности на этапе компиляции. Экспериментальные результаты показывают значительное улучшение: pass rate повышается с 21.1% до 33.3% на Claude Code, с 35.1% до 48.7% на Kimi CLI, плюс экономия токенов на 10-46% и обнаружение 94.8% потенциальных уязвимостей.

en.title: Streamlining Agent Skills with SkCC: Secure and Portable Deployment
en.desc: SkCC is a framework designed to improve the deployment of agent skills across various platforms by using a strongly-typed intermediate representation called SkIR. This approach separates the skill's functionality from the specific formatting required by different platforms, which helps in reducing the complexity of adapting skills. Additionally, SkCC includes a compile-time Analyzer that ensures security by preventing vulnerabilities before deployment. The framework has shown significant performance improvements in skill execution and reduced maintenance burdens, making it easier to manage agent capabilities across diverse environments.

zh.title: SkCC：提升代理技能的安全性与性能
zh.desc: SkCC是一个编译框架，利用强类型中间表示来实现代理技能在不同平台上的可移植部署，同时确保安全性和提高性能。该框架通过引入经典编译器设计，解决了不同代理框架对提示格式的敏感性问题，从而减少了适配复杂性。SkIR作为核心的中间表示，解耦了技能语义与平台特定格式，使得技能可以在异构代理框架中便捷部署。实验结果表明，编译后的技能在性能上显著优于原始版本，且在安全性和运行效率上也有显著提升。

papers.title: ModelLens: Finding the Best for Your Task from Myriads of Models
papers.abstract: Abstract ModelLens is a unified framework that recommends models in real-world scenarios by learning from public leaderboard data to rank unseen models on unseen datasets without requiring costly evaluations.  AI-generated summary The open-source model ecosystem now contains hundreds of thousands of pretrained models, yet picking the best model for a new dataset is increasingly infeasible: new models and unbenchmarked datasets emerge continuously, leaving practitioners with no prior records on either side. Existing approaches handle only fragments of this in-the-wild setting: AutoML and transferability estimation select models from small predefined pools or require expensive per-model forward passes on the target dataset, while model routing presupposes a given candidate pool. We introduce ModelLens, a unified framework for model recommendation in the wild. Our key insight is that public leaderboard interactions, though scattered and noisy, collectively trace out an implicit atlas of model capabilities across heterogeneous evaluation settings, a signal rich enough to learn from directly. By learning a performance-aware latent space over model--dataset--metric tuples, ModelLens ranks unseen models on unseen datasets without running candidates on the target dataset. On a new benchmark of 1.62M evaluation records spanning 47K models and 9.6K datasets, ModelLens surpasses baselines that either rely on metadata alone or require running each candidate on the target dataset. Its recommended Top-K pools further improve multiple representative routing methods by up to 81% across diverse QA benchmarks. Case studies on recently released benchmarks further confirm generalization to both text and vision-language tasks.

ru.title: Рекомендация моделей через неявный атлас возможностей из общественных лидербордов
ru.desc: ModelLens представляет собой единую систему для рекомендации моделей машинного обучения в реальных сценариях, которая учится из общедоступных данных лидербордов, чтобы ранжировать неизвестные модели на неизвестных датасетах без дорогостоящих вычислений. Ключевая идея заключается в том, что взаимодействия в общественных лидербордах содержат неявную информацию о возможностях моделей на различных задачах, которую можно использовать для обучения. Метод обучает латентное пространство перформанса над кортежами модель-датасет-метрика, позволяя прогнозировать производительность без запуска кандидатов на целевом датасете. Фреймворк продемонстрировал превосходство над базовыми подходами на бенчмарке из 1.62M оценок, охватывающих 47K моделей и 9.6K датасетов.

en.title: ModelLens: Smart Model Recommendations Without Costly Evaluations
en.desc: ModelLens is a novel framework designed to recommend machine learning models for new datasets by leveraging data from public leaderboards. It addresses the challenge of selecting the best model from a vast pool of pretrained models without the need for expensive evaluations on each candidate. By creating a performance-aware latent space that captures the relationships between models, datasets, and metrics, ModelLens can effectively rank unseen models for unseen datasets. The framework has demonstrated significant improvements over existing methods, achieving better performance in diverse tasks across text and vision-language benchmarks.

zh.title: ModelLens：智能模型推荐的新视野
zh.desc: ModelLens 是一个统一的框架，通过学习公共排行榜数据来推荐模型，能够在没有昂贵评估的情况下对未见过的模型进行排名。该框架利用公共排行榜的互动信息，构建出一个隐含的模型能力图谱，从而直接学习模型在不同评估设置下的表现。ModelLens 在一个包含 162 万条评估记录的基准测试中，超越了依赖元数据或需要在目标数据集上运行每个候选模型的基线方法。它的推荐 Top-K 模型池在多个代表性路由方法上提高了多达 81% 的性能，证明了其在文本和视觉语言任务中的广泛适用性。

papers.title: InterLV-Search: Benchmarking Interleaved Multimodal Agentic Search
papers.abstract: Abstract InterLV-Search benchmark evaluates interleaved language-vision agentic search by repeatedly using textual and visual evidence to condition later search, revealing current systems' limitations in visual evidence seeking and multimodal evidence integration.  AI-generated summary Existing benchmarks for multimodal agentic search evaluate multimodal search and visual browsing, but visual evidence is either confined to the input or treated as an answer endpoint rather than part of an interleaved search trajectory. We introduce InterLV-Search, a benchmark for Interleaved Language-Vision Agentic Search, in which textual and visual evidence is repeatedly used to condition later search. It contains 2,061 examples across three levels: active visual evidence seeking, controlled offline interleaved multimodal search, and open-web interleaved multimodal search. Beyond existing benchmarks, it also includes multimodal multi-branch samples that involve comparison between multiple entities during the evidence search. We construct Level 1 and Level 2 with automated pipelines and Level 3 with a machine-led, human-supervised open-web pipeline. We further provide InterLV-Agent for standardized tool use, trajectory logging, and evaluation. Experiments on proprietary and open-source multimodal agents show that current systems remain far from solving interleaved multimodal search, with the best model below 50% overall accuracy, highlighting challenges in visual evidence seeking, search control, and multimodal evidence integration. We release the benchmark data and evaluation code at https://github.com/hbhalpha/InterLV-Search-Bench
affiliations: Southern University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Переплетение текста и изображений: оценка интеллектуального мультимодального поиска
ru.desc: В работе представлен бенчмарк InterLV-Search для оценки агентских систем мультимодального поиска, которые чередуют использование текстовой и визуальной информации для последовательного уточнения поисковых запросов. Бенчмарк содержит 2061 пример, включая задачи активного поиска визуальных доказательств, контролируемый оффлайн поиск и поиск в открытом интернете, а также мультивещественные примеры для сравнения нескольких объектов. Авторы показали, что современные мультимодальные агенты достигают точности ниже 50%, выявив критические проблемы в поиске визуальных доказательств, управлении поиском и интеграции мультимодальной информации. Исследование демонстрирует необходимость развития более совершенных методов для обработки взаимосвязанной последовательности текстовых и визуальных сигналов в задачах агентского поиска.

en.title: Revolutionizing Multimodal Search with InterLV-Search
en.desc: The paper introduces the InterLV-Search benchmark, which evaluates how well AI systems can perform interleaved language-vision searches using both text and images. Unlike previous benchmarks, this new framework allows for continuous use of visual evidence throughout the search process rather than just at the beginning or end. It includes a diverse set of examples that challenge current models to improve their ability to seek visual evidence and integrate multimodal information effectively. The results show that existing systems struggle with these tasks, achieving less than 50% accuracy, indicating significant room for improvement in multimodal search capabilities.

zh.title: 交错语言-视觉智能搜索的新基准
zh.desc: InterLV-Search基准测试评估交错的语言-视觉智能搜索，强调了当前系统在视觉证据寻求和多模态证据整合方面的局限性。该基准包含2061个示例，分为三个层次，涵盖主动视觉证据寻求、受控的离线交错多模态搜索和开放网络交错多模态搜索。与现有基准不同，它还包括多模态多分支样本，涉及在证据搜索过程中对多个实体的比较。实验结果表明，当前系统在解决交错多模态搜索方面仍然面临重大挑战，最佳模型的整体准确率低于50%。

papers.title: Fast Byte Latent Transformer
papers.abstract: Abstract Byte-level language models overcome slow autoregressive generation through diffusion-based parallel processing and speculative decoding techniques that improve both speed and quality.  AI-generated summary Recent byte-level language models (LMs) match the performance of token-level models without relying on subword vocabularies, yet their utility is limited by slow, byte-by-byte autoregressive generation. We address this bottleneck in the Byte Latent Transformer (BLT) through new training and generation techniques. First, we introduce BLT Diffusion (BLT-D), a new model and our fastest BLT variant, trained with an auxiliary block-wise diffusion objective alongside the standard next-byte prediction loss. This enables an inference procedure that generates multiple bytes in parallel per decoding step, substantially reducing the number of forward passes required to generate a sequence. Second, we propose two extensions inspired by speculative decoding that trade some of this speed for higher generation quality: BLT Self-speculation (BLT-S), in which BLT's local decoder continues generating past its normal patch boundaries to draft bytes, which are then verified with a single full-model forward pass; and BLT Diffusion+Verification (BLT-DV), which augments BLT-D with an autoregressive verification step after diffusion-based generation. All methods may achieve an estimated memory-bandwidth cost over 50% lower than BLT on generation tasks. Each approach offers its own unique advantages, together removing key barriers to the practical use of byte-level LMs.

ru.title: Параллельная генерация текста в байт-уровневых моделях через диффузию и верификацию
ru.desc: В работе предлагаются методы ускорения автарегрессивной генерации текста в байт-уровневых языковых моделях. Основной подход BLT Diffusion использует вспомогательную блочную диффузионную цель при обучении, что позволяет генерировать несколько байтов параллельно на каждом шаге декодирования. Авторы также представляют два расширения, вдохновленные спекулятивным декодированием: BLT Self-speculation, где локальный декодер генерирует драфт-байты за границами патча, и BLT Diffusion+Verification, объединяющий диффузионную генерацию с аутарегрессивной верификацией. Все предложенные методы снижают пиковое потребление памяти на более чем 50% по сравнению с базовой моделью при сохранении или улучшении качества генерации.

en.title: Speed and Quality Revolution in Byte-Level Language Models
en.desc: This paper presents the Byte Latent Transformer (BLT), a new type of byte-level language model that improves the speed and quality of text generation. It introduces BLT Diffusion (BLT-D), which allows for parallel byte generation, significantly speeding up the process compared to traditional autoregressive methods. Additionally, the paper explores two techniques, BLT Self-speculation and BLT Diffusion+Verification, which enhance generation quality by incorporating verification steps. Overall, these innovations reduce memory bandwidth costs and make byte-level models more practical for real-world applications.

zh.title: 提升字节级语言模型生成速度与质量的创新方法
zh.desc: 本文介绍了一种新的字节级语言模型，称为字节潜在变换器（BLT），旨在解决传统自回归生成速度慢的问题。通过引入BLT扩散（BLT-D）模型，采用辅助的块级扩散目标，允许在解码步骤中并行生成多个字节，从而显著减少生成序列所需的前向传递次数。此外，文章还提出了两种基于推测解码的扩展方法，分别是BLT自我推测（BLT-S）和BLT扩散+验证（BLT-DV），以提高生成质量。所有方法在生成任务中都能实现超过50%的内存带宽成本降低，推动了字节级语言模型的实际应用。

papers.title: From Storage to Experience: A Survey on the Evolution of LLM Agent Memory Mechanisms
papers.abstract: Abstract Large language model agents rely on memory mechanisms that evolve through three stages—storage, reflection, and experience—driven by consistency, dynamic environments, and continual learning goals.  AI-generated summary Large Language Model (LLM)-based agents have fundamentally reshaped artificial intelligence by integrating external tools and planning capabilities. While memory mechanisms have emerged as the architectural cornerstone of these systems, current research remains fragmented, oscillating between operating system engineering and cognitive science. This theoretical divide prevents a unified view of technological synthesis and a coherent evolutionary perspective. To bridge this gap, this survey proposes a novel evolutionary framework for LLM agent memory mechanisms, formalizing the development process into three stages: Storage (trajectory preservation), Reflection (trajectory refinement), and Experience (trajectory abstraction). We first formally define these three stages before analyzing the three core drivers of this evolution: the necessity for long-range consistency, the challenges in dynamic environments, and the ultimate goal of continual learning. Furthermore, we specifically explore two transformative mechanisms in the frontier Experience stage: proactive exploration and cross-trajectory abstraction. By synthesizing these disparate views, this work offers robust design principles and a clear roadmap for the development of next-generation LLM agents.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology
University of Science and Technology Beijing

ru.title: Эволюция памяти в LLM-агентах: от хранения к абстрагированию опыта
ru.desc: В этой работе предложена эволюционная框架для механизмов памяти LLM-агентов, которые развиваются через три стадии: Storage (сохранение траекторий), Reflection (уточнение траекторий) и Experience (абстрагирование траекторий). Авторы определяют три ключевых движущих фактора эволюции: необходимость долгосрочной консистентности, адаптация к динамическим окружениям и достижение постоянного обучения. Особое внимание уделяется передовым механизмам на стадии Experience: проактивной разведке и кросс-траекторному абстрагированию. Работа объединяет разрозненные подходы из инженерии и когнитивной науки, предоставляя принципы проектирования и дорожную карту для разработки агентов нового поколения.

en.title: Evolving Memory Mechanisms for Next-Gen LLM Agents
en.desc: This paper discusses how large language model (LLM) agents use memory in three key stages: Storage, Reflection, and Experience. These stages help LLMs maintain consistency, adapt to changing environments, and achieve continual learning. The authors propose a new framework that connects insights from both engineering and cognitive science to improve LLM design. By focusing on proactive exploration and cross-trajectory abstraction, the paper aims to guide the development of more advanced LLM agents.

zh.title: 大语言模型代理的记忆机制进化框架
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了大语言模型代理的记忆机制，分为存储、反思和经验三个阶段。记忆机制是这些系统的核心，影响着它们的学习和适应能力。论文提出了一个新的进化框架，强调了长远一致性、动态环境中的挑战和持续学习的目标。通过整合不同的观点，提供了下一代大语言模型代理的设计原则和发展路线图。

papers.title: LiVeAction: a Lightweight, Versatile, and Asymmetric Neural Codec Design for Real-time Operation
papers.abstract: Abstract LiVeAction is a lightweight neural codec architecture that improves rate-distortion performance for resource-constrained devices by using an FFT-like structure and variance-based rate penalty instead of adversarial losses.  AI-generated summary Modern sensors generate rich, high-fidelity data, yet applications operating on wearable or remote sensing devices remain constrained by bandwidth and power budgets. Standardized codecs such as JPEG and MPEG achieve efficient trade-offs between bitrate and perceptual quality but are designed for human perception, limiting their applicability to machine-perception tasks and non-traditional modalities such as spatial audio arrays, hyperspectral images, and 3D medical images. General-purpose compression schemes based on scalar quantization or resolution reduction are broadly applicable but fail to exploit inherent signal redundancies, resulting in suboptimal rate-distortion performance. Recent generative neural codecs, or tokenizers, model complex signal dependencies but are often over-parameterized, data-hungry, and modality-specific, making them impractical for resource-constrained environments. We introduce a Lightweight, Versatile, and Asymmetric neural codec architecture (LiVeAction), that addresses these limitations through two key ideas. (1) To reduce the complexity of the encoder to meet the resource constraints of the execution environments, we impose an FFT-like structure and reduce the overall size and depth of the neural-network-based analysis transform. (2) To allow arbitrary signal modalities and simplify training, we replace adversarial and perceptual losses with a variance-based rate penalty. Our design produces codecs that deliver superior rate-distortion performance compared to state-of-the-art generative tokenizers, while remaining practical for deployment on low-power sensors. We release our code, experiments, and python library at https://github.com/UT-SysML/liveaction .

ru.title: Компактный нейронный кодек для устройств с ограниченной мощностью
ru.desc: LiVeAction — это лёгкая архитектура нейронного кодека, предназначенная для сжатия данных на устройствах с ограниченными ресурсами. Авторы используют FFT-подобную структуру в энкодере для снижения вычислительной сложности и заменяют adversarial-потери на штраф на основе дисперсии, что упрощает обучение и делает метод универсальным для разных модальностей сигналов. Предложенный подход достигает лучшего баланса между битрейтом и качеством по сравнению с современными генеративными кодеками, оставаясь практичным для развёртывания на маломощных датчиках и мобильных устройствах. Кодек эффективно работает с разнообразными типами данных, включая гиперспектральные изображения, пространственный звук и 3D медицинские снимки.

en.title: Efficient Neural Codec for Resource-Constrained Devices
en.desc: LiVeAction is a novel neural codec architecture designed to enhance rate-distortion performance for devices with limited resources. It utilizes an FFT-like structure to simplify the encoder, making it less complex and more efficient for low-power environments. Instead of relying on adversarial losses, it employs a variance-based rate penalty, allowing it to handle various signal types effectively. This approach results in superior performance compared to existing generative tokenizers, making it suitable for applications in wearable and remote sensing technologies.

zh.title: 轻量级神经编解码器，提升资源受限设备性能
zh.desc: LiVeAction是一种轻量级神经编解码器架构，旨在提高资源受限设备的比特率-失真性能。它采用类似FFT的结构和基于方差的速率惩罚，取代了对抗损失，从而降低了编码器的复杂性。该架构能够处理多种信号模态，简化了训练过程，并且在低功耗传感器上实现了优越的性能。与现有的生成性编码器相比，LiVeAction在保持高效性的同时，适应了资源受限环境的需求。

papers.title: MDN: Parallelizing Stepwise Momentum for Delta Linear Attention
papers.abstract: Abstract Linear attention models face challenges with information decay and convergence, which are addressed through a momentum-based approach that improves training efficiency and performance over existing models like Mamba2 and GDN.  AI-generated summary Linear Attention (LA) offers a promising paradigm for scaling large language models (LLMs) to long sequences by avoiding the quadratic complexity of self-attention. Recent LA models such as Mamba2 and GDN interpret linear recurrences as closed-form online stochastic gradient descent (SGD), but naive SGD updates suffer from rapid information decay and suboptimal convergence in optimization. While momentum-based optimizers provide a natural remedy, they pose challenges in simultaneously achieving training efficiency and effectiveness. To address this, we develop a chunkwise parallel algorithm for LA with a stepwise momentum rule by geometrically reordering the update coefficients. Further, from a dynamical systems perspective, we analyze the momentum-based recurrence as a second-order system that introduces complex conjugate eigenvalues. This analysis guides the design of stable gating constraints. The resulting model, Momentum DeltaNet (MDN), leverages Triton kernels to achieve comparable training throughput with competitive linear models such as Mamba2 and KDA. Extensive experiments on the 400M and 1.3B parameter models demonstrate consistent performance improvements over strong baselines, including Transformers, Mamba2 and GDN, across diverse downstream evaluation benchmarks. Code: https://github.com/HuuYuLong/MomentumDeltaNet .
affiliations: The Hong Kong University of Science and Technology (Guangzhou), Guangzhou, China

ru.title: Momentum для линейного внимания: стабильность через импульс
ru.desc: В работе предложен новый подход к линейным моделям внимания, который решает проблемы потери информации и медленной сходимости, используя импульс (momentum) в оптимизаторе. Авторы разработали параллельный алгоритм с пошаговым правилом momentum и геометрической переупорядочкой коэффициентов обновления для эффективного обучения. Анализ модели с точки зрения динамических систем показал, что momentum вводит комплексные собственные значения, на основе чего были спроектированы стабильные ограничения. Полученная модель Momentum DeltaNet (MDN) демонстрирует лучшую производительность чем Mamba2, GDN и Transformers на моделях размером 400M и 1.3B параметров.

en.title: Momentum DeltaNet: Enhancing Linear Attention with Momentum for Better Performance
en.desc: This paper introduces Momentum DeltaNet (MDN), a linear attention model that enhances training efficiency and performance by addressing issues of information decay and convergence. The authors propose a chunkwise parallel algorithm that utilizes a stepwise momentum rule, improving the optimization process compared to traditional methods. By analyzing the model from a dynamical systems perspective, they ensure stability in the update process through the introduction of gating constraints. Extensive experiments show that MDN outperforms existing models like Mamba2 and GDN on various benchmarks, demonstrating its effectiveness in scaling large language models.

zh.title: 动量优化，提升线性注意力模型性能
zh.desc: 线性注意力模型在信息衰减和收敛方面面临挑战。本文提出了一种基于动量的方法，旨在提高训练效率和性能，超越现有模型如Mamba2和GDN。我们开发了一种分块并行算法，结合逐步动量规则，优化了更新系数的几何排序。最终的Momentum DeltaNet模型在多个基准测试中表现出色，显示出相较于强基线模型的持续性能提升。

papers.title: SpecBlock: Block-Iterative Speculative Decoding with Dynamic Tree Drafting
papers.abstract: Abstract Speculative decoding with SpecBlock combines block-iterative drafting and path dependence to improve LLM inference speed while maintaining accuracy through adaptive mechanisms.  AI-generated summary Speculative decoding accelerates LLM inference by drafting a tree of candidate continuations and verifying it in one target forward. Existing drafters fall into two camps with opposite weaknesses. Autoregressive drafters such as EAGLE-3 preserve dependence along each draft path but call the drafter once per tree depth, making drafting a non-trivial share of per-iteration latency. Parallel drafters cut drafter calls by predicting multiple future positions in one forward, but each position is predicted without seeing the others, producing paths the verifier rejects. In this paper, we propose SpecBlock, a block-iterative drafter that combines path dependence with cheap drafting. Each drafter forward produces K dependent positions and we call this a block. The draft tree grows through repeated block expansions. Two mechanisms explicitly carry path dependence to keep later draft positions accurate. Within each block, a layer-wise shift carries the previous position's hidden state into every decoder layer. Across blocks, each new block can start from any position of the previous block, inheriting its hidden state to extend the path. To spend verifier budget where acceptance is likely, a co-trained rank head replaces the fixed top-k tree by allocating per-position branching during drafting. To avoid training the drafter on prefixes it never produces at inference, a valid-prefix mask drops the loss at later positions once an earlier one is wrong. Beyond static drafting, a cost-aware bandit at deployment uses free verifier feedback to update the drafter selectively, only when the expected throughput gain exceeds the update cost. Experiments show that SpecBlock improves mean speedup by 8-13% over EAGLE-3 at 44-52% of its drafting cost, and cost-aware adaptation extends this lead to 11-19%.
affiliations: Hong Kong University of Science and Technology

ru.title: Быстрое предложение с памятью: блок-итеративное спекулятивное декодирование
ru.desc: SpecBlock — это метод спекулятивного декодирования для ускорения инференса больших языковых моделей, который комбинирует блок-итеративное предложение кандидатов с сохранением зависимостей вдоль путей генерации. В отличие от существующих подходов, которые либо медленно генерируют последовательно, либо быстро, но независимо предсказывают множество позиций, SpecBlock производит K зависимых позиций за один проход черезз передачу скрытых состояний между слоями и блоками. Адаптивный ранжирующий модуль и маска валидных префиксов оптимизируют распределение ветвлений в дереве поиска и избегают обучения на неправдоподобных последовательностях. Эксперименты показывают ускорение на 8-19% по сравнению с EAGLE-3 при значительно меньших вычислительных затратах на черновик.

en.title: Boosting LLM Speed with Smart Drafting
en.desc: The paper introduces SpecBlock, a novel approach to speculative decoding that enhances the inference speed of large language models (LLMs) while preserving accuracy. It combines block-iterative drafting with path dependence, allowing the model to generate multiple candidate continuations in a single forward pass. By using mechanisms that maintain the hidden state across drafts, SpecBlock ensures that later predictions are informed by earlier ones, improving the quality of the generated paths. The method also incorporates a cost-aware bandit strategy to adapt the drafter based on real-time feedback, leading to significant speed improvements over existing methods.

zh.title: 推测解码：加速LLM推理的新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种名为SpecBlock的推测解码方法，旨在提高大型语言模型（LLM）的推理速度，同时保持准确性。SpecBlock结合了块迭代草拟和路径依赖的机制，通过生成候选继续的树状结构来加速推理过程。与现有的自回归草拟器和并行草拟器相比，SpecBlock在每个块内生成多个依赖位置，并通过层级状态传递和块间状态继承来保持路径的准确性。实验结果表明，SpecBlock在草拟成本的44-52%下，平均加速提高了8-13%，并通过成本感知适应进一步提升了这一优势。

papers.title: Steering Visual Generation in Unified Multimodal Models with Understanding Supervision
papers.abstract: Abstract Understanding-oriented post-training framework enhances generative models by using comprehension tasks as supervisory signals for improved image generation and editing.  AI-generated summary Unified multimodal models are envisioned to bridge the gap between understanding and generation. Yet, to achieve competitive performance, state-of-the-art models adopt largely decoupled understanding and generation components. This design, while effective for individual tasks, weakens the connection required for mutual enhancement, leaving the potential synergy empirically uncertain. We propose to explicitly restore this synergy by introducing Understanding-Oriented Post-Training (UNO), a lightweight framework that treats understanding not only as a distinct task, but also a direct supervisory signal to steer generative representations. By incorporating objectives that encode semantic abstraction (captioning) and structural details (visual regression), we enable effective gradient flow from understanding to generation. Extensive experiments on image generation and editing demonstrate that understanding can serve as an effective catalyst for generation.

ru.title: Понимание как учитель генерации: объединение зрения и синтеза
ru.desc: В этой работе предлагается фреймворк Understanding-Oriented Post-Training (UNO), который объединяет задачи понимания изображений с генеративными моделями. Авторы показывают, что задачи компьютерного зрения, такие как генерация описаний и визуальная регрессия, могут служить сигналами для обучения генеративных компонент. Через явное соединение путей градиентов между пониманием и генерацией удается значительно улучшить качество синтеза и редактирования изображений. Исследование подтверждает, что понимание визуального контента действительно может эффективно усиливать способности генеративных моделей к созданию и модификации изображений.

en.title: Bridging Understanding and Generation for Better AI Images
en.desc: The paper introduces the Understanding-Oriented Post-Training (UNO) framework, which enhances generative models by using understanding tasks as guiding signals for better image generation and editing. It addresses the issue of decoupled understanding and generation components in existing models, which limits their performance. By integrating understanding tasks like captioning and visual regression, UNO allows for improved gradient flow from understanding to generation. The results show that this approach effectively boosts the quality of AI-generated images by leveraging the synergy between understanding and generation.

zh.title: 理解促进生成的创新框架
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种理解导向的后训练框架（UNO），旨在通过理解任务作为监督信号来增强生成模型的性能。该框架将理解视为不仅是独立任务，还直接影响生成表示的信号，从而恢复理解与生成之间的协同作用。通过引入编码语义抽象（如图像描述）和结构细节（如视觉回归）的目标，促进了理解到生成的有效梯度流。实验结果表明，理解可以有效地促进生成过程，提升图像生成和编辑的效果。

papers.title: Empirical Evidence for Simply Connected Decision Regions in Image Classifiers
papers.abstract: Abstract Decision regions in deep neural networks exhibit simple connectivity, demonstrated through quad-mesh filling procedures and Coons patch analysis.  AI-generated summary Understanding the topology of decision regions is central to explaining the inner workings of deep neural networks. Prior empirical work has provided evidence that these regions are path connected. We study a stronger topological question: whether closed loops inside a decision region can be contracted without leaving that region. To this end, we propose an iterative quad-mesh filling procedure that constructs a finite-resolution label-preserving surface bounded by a given loop and lying entirely within the same decision region. We further connect this construction to natural Coons patches in order to quantify its deviation from a canonical geometric interpolation of the loop. By evaluating our method across several modern image-classification models, we provide empirical evidence supporting the hypothesis that decision regions in deep neural networks are not only path connected, but also simply connected.

ru.title: Простая связность областей решения в глубоких нейронных сетях
ru.desc: В работе исследуется топология областей принятия решений в глубоких нейронных сетях. Авторы предлагают итеративный алгоритм заполнения четырёхугольной сетки, который строит поверхность внутри единой области решения, ограниченную замкнутым контуром. Метод связывает эту конструкцию с патчами Кунса для анализа отклонения от каноничной геометрической интерполяции. На примере современных моделей классификации изображений демонстрируется, что области решения в нейронных сетях не только связны по путям, но и просто связны.

en.title: Exploring Simple Connectivity in Neural Network Decision Regions
en.desc: This paper investigates the topology of decision regions in deep neural networks, focusing on their connectivity properties. The authors propose a method to analyze whether closed loops within these regions can be contracted without leaving the region, indicating a stronger form of connectivity known as simple connectivity. They introduce an iterative quad-mesh filling procedure to create a surface that remains within the decision region while preserving labels. Through experiments on various image-classification models, the study provides evidence that these decision regions are not only path connected but also simply connected, enhancing our understanding of neural network behavior.

zh.title: 深度神经网络的决策区域：简单连通性探究
zh.desc: 深度神经网络中的决策区域表现出简单的连通性，这通过四边形网格填充程序和Coons补丁分析得以证明。理解决策区域的拓扑结构对于解释深度神经网络的内部工作原理至关重要。我们提出了一种迭代的四边形网格填充程序，构建了一个有限分辨率的标签保持表面，该表面完全位于同一决策区域内。通过对多个现代图像分类模型的评估，我们提供了实证证据，支持深度神经网络中的决策区域不仅是路径连通的，而且是简单连通的。

papers.title: Discovering Reinforcement Learning Interfaces with Large Language Models
papers.abstract: Abstract Automated reinforcement learning interface discovery using LLM-guided evolutionary algorithms that jointly optimize observation mappings and reward functions from raw simulator state.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning systems rely on environment interfaces that specify observations and reward functions, yet constructing these interfaces for new tasks often requires substantial manual effort. While recent work has automated reward design using large language models (LLMs), these approaches assume fixed observations and do not address the broader challenge of synthesizing complete task interfaces. We study RL task interface discovery from raw simulator state, where both observation mappings and reward functions must be generated. We propose LIMEN (Code available at https://github.com/Lossfunk/LIMEN), a LLM guided evolutionary framework that produces candidate interfaces as executable programs and iteratively refines them using policy training feedback. Across novel discrete gridworld tasks and continuous control domains spanning locomotion and manipulation, joint evolution of observations and rewards discovers effective interfaces given only a trajectory-level success metric, while optimizing either component alone fails on at least one domain. These results demonstrate that automatic construction of RL interfaces from raw state can substantially reduce manual engineering and that observation and reward components often benefit from co-design, as single-component optimization fails catastrophically on at least one domain in our evaluation suite.

ru.title: Совместная эволюция наблюдений и наград для автоматического открытия интерфейсов RL
ru.desc: Статья представляет LIMEN — фреймворк для автоматического открытия интерфейсов задач обучения с подкреплением, используя большие языковые модели и эволюционные алгоритмы. Система одновременно оптимизирует отображения наблюдений и функции награды на основе сырого состояния симулятора, представляя кандидатов как исполняемые программы. Авторы демонстрируют, что совместная эволюция компонентов наблюдений и наград превосходит оптимизацию отдельных компонентов на различных задачах — от дискретных лабиринтов до непрерывного управления. Работа показывает, что автоматическое конструирование интерфейсов существенно снижает ручное проектирование и подчеркивает важность совместного дизайна компонентов.

en.title: Automating RL Interface Discovery with Co-Designed Observations and Rewards
en.desc: This paper presents LIMEN, a novel framework that automates the discovery of reinforcement learning (RL) interfaces by using large language models (LLMs) and evolutionary algorithms. It addresses the challenge of generating both observation mappings and reward functions from raw simulator states, which traditionally requires significant manual effort. The framework iteratively refines candidate interfaces through policy training feedback, demonstrating that joint optimization of observations and rewards leads to more effective task interfaces. The results show that co-designing these components is crucial, as optimizing them separately can lead to failures in certain domains.

zh.title: 自动化强化学习接口发现的创新方法
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种自动化的强化学习接口发现方法，利用大型语言模型（LLM）指导的进化算法，优化观察映射和奖励函数。传统的强化学习系统需要手动构建环境接口，而我们的研究旨在从原始模拟器状态中自动生成这些接口。我们提出的LIMEN框架能够生成可执行的候选接口，并通过策略训练反馈进行迭代优化。实验结果表明，联合进化观察和奖励可以有效发现接口，显著减少手动工程的需求。

papers.title: Who Prices Cognitive Labor in the Age of Agents? Compute-Anchored Wages
papers.abstract: Abstract AI agents function as a production technology converting compute capital into cognitive labor, shifting the wage-setting mechanism from labor markets to compute capital markets.  AI-generated summary A natural intuition about the economics of AI agents is that, because agents can be replicated at very low marginal cost, agent labor may be supplied highly elastically, placing downward pressure on cognitive-labor wages when it closely substitutes for human labor. We argue this framing is wrong in mechanism but partially correct in conclusion, and that the correction matters for both theory and policy. Agents are not labor; they are a production technology that converts compute capital K_c into effective units of cognitive labor L_A. Once this is recognized, the elastic-supply margin that anchors the equilibrium wage migrates from the labor market to the compute capital market. Building on the classic factor-pricing framework mankiw2020, we derive a Compute-Anchored Wage (CAW) bound stating that, on tasks where human and agent-produced cognitive labor are substitutes, the competitive human wage is bounded above by λcdot k cdot r_c, where r_c is the rental rate of compute capital, k is the compute intensity of one effective agent-produced cognitive labor unit, and λ is the relative human-to-agent productivity. We generalize the result through constant elasticity of substitution (CES) aggregation, separate substitutable from complementary tasks, and discuss factor-share consequences. The conclusion is concise: the price-setter for cognitive labor is no longer the labor market.

ru.title: Когда ценообразование труда зависит от цены GPU
ru.desc: В работе авторы переосмысляют экономику AI-агентов, утверждая, что они не являются трудовыми ресурсами, а представляют собой производственную технологию, конвертирующую вычислительный капитал в когнитивный труд. Они выводят границу Compute-Anchored Wage (CAW), которая связывает заработную плату человека с ценой вычислительных ресурсов, интенсивностью вычислений и относительной производительностью. Анализ проводится в рамках классического фреймворка ценообразования факторов производства с использованием CES-функций для разделения задач на взаимозаменяемые и дополняющие. Ключевой вывод: рыночные механизмы определения цены когнитивного труда смещаются из рынка труда на рынок вычислительного капитала.

en.title: AI Agents: Redefining Wage Dynamics through Compute Capital
en.desc: This paper discusses how AI agents act as a new form of production technology that transforms compute capital into cognitive labor. It argues that instead of AI agents being treated as labor, they should be seen as a technology that influences wage-setting through compute capital markets. The authors introduce the concept of Compute-Anchored Wage (CAW), which indicates that human wages are now influenced by the cost of compute capital rather than traditional labor market dynamics. This shift has important implications for economic theory and policy regarding the relationship between human and AI-generated labor.

zh.title: 人工智能代理：重新定义认知劳动的工资机制
zh.desc: 这篇论文探讨了人工智能代理作为一种生产技术的作用，它将计算资本转化为认知劳动。由于代理可以以极低的边际成本复制，因此它们的劳动供应可能会非常灵活，从而对人类劳动的工资产生下行压力。作者认为，代理并不是劳动，而是一种将计算资本转化为有效认知劳动的技术。这一认识改变了工资的平衡机制，使其从劳动市场转向计算资本市场。

papers.title: Rethinking RL for LLM Reasoning: It's Sparse Policy Selection, Not Capability Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Reinforcement learning in language models primarily corrects uncertainty at specific decision points rather than acquiring new capabilities, enabling a more efficient RL-free approach called ReasonMaxxer that achieves comparable performance with significantly reduced training costs.  AI-generated summary Reinforcement learning has become the standard for improving reasoning in large language models, yet evidence increasingly suggests that RL does not teach new strategies; it redistributes probability mass over solutions the base model already contains. In this work, we ask: if RL merely steers the model toward paths it already knows, is the RL optimization loop itself necessary? Through token-level analysis across multiple model families and RL algorithms, we find that RL's beneficial footprint is a sparse, predictable correction concentrated at high-entropy decision points where the model is uncertain which branch to take. Only 1--3\% of token positions are affected, the promoted token always lies within the base model's top-5 alternatives, and targeted corrections at those few positions causally recover a large fraction of RL's accuracy gain, while random corrections fail. The base model's own entropy identifies these positions without any RL-trained model, and the entire correction is low-dimensional, representable in a tiny fraction of model parameters. These findings reframe reasoning improvement as sparse policy selection, not capability acquisition. We translate this insight into ReasonMaxxer, a minimal RL-free method that applies contrastive loss only at entropy-gated decision points, using a few hundred base-model rollouts and no online generation. Across three model families, six scales, and six math reasoning benchmarks, ReasonMaxxer matches or exceeds full RL performance while requiring only tens of problems and minutes of single-GPU training, a reduction in training cost of roughly three orders of magnitude.

ru.title: Избавляемся от обучения с подкреплением: умная коррекция неопределённости вместо переобучения
ru.desc: В этой работе показано, что обучение с подкреплением в языковых моделях не учит новые стратегии, а перераспределяет вероятности между уже известными решениями. Авторы выяснили, что RL воздействует только на 1-3% позиций токенов в местах высокой неопределённости, где модель не знает, какой путь выбрать. На основе этого открытия предложен метод ReasonMaxxer, который достигает качества полного RL-обучения без самого процесса обучения с подкреплением. Новый подход использует контрастивные потери только в критических точках неопределённости и требует в тысячи раз меньше вычислительных ресурсов.

en.title: Efficient Reasoning with ReasonMaxxer: Sparsity Over Complexity
en.desc: This paper explores the role of reinforcement learning (RL) in enhancing reasoning capabilities of language models. It argues that RL primarily fine-tunes existing knowledge rather than teaching new strategies, focusing on specific decision points where the model is uncertain. The authors introduce ReasonMaxxer, a novel approach that uses a minimal RL-free method to improve model performance by applying corrections only at high-entropy decision points. This method significantly reduces training costs while achieving comparable or better results than traditional RL methods.

zh.title: 无强化学习的高效推理提升方法
zh.desc: 本论文探讨了在语言模型中强化学习的作用，发现其主要是在特定决策点修正不确定性，而不是获取新能力。研究提出了一种名为ReasonMaxxer的方法，这是一种无强化学习的高效方法，能够在显著降低训练成本的同时，达到与传统强化学习相当的性能。通过对多个模型和算法的分析，发现强化学习的有效性集中在少数高熵决策点，只有1-3%的标记位置受到影响。最终，ReasonMaxxer通过在这些决策点应用对比损失，成功实现了推理能力的提升，而无需复杂的强化学习过程。

papers.title: PrefixGuard: From LLM-Agent Traces to Online Failure-Warning Monitors
papers.abstract: Abstract PrefixGuard enables effective online monitoring of LLM agents through trace analysis and prefix-based risk scoring, demonstrating strong performance across multiple benchmark tasks while providing diagnostic insights for alert reliability.  AI-generated summary Large language model (LLM) agents now execute long, tool-using tasks where final outcome checks can arrive too late for intervention. Online warning requires lightweight prefix monitors over heterogeneous traces, but hand-authored event schemas are brittle and deployment-time LLM judging is costly. We introduce PrefixGuard, a trace-to-monitor framework with an offline StepView induction step followed by supervised monitor training. StepView induces deterministic typed-step adapters from raw trace samples, and the monitor learns an event abstraction and prefix-risk scorer from terminal outcomes. Across WebArena, τ^2-Bench, SkillsBench, and TerminalBench, the strongest PrefixGuard monitors reach 0.900/0.710/0.533/0.557 AUPRC. Using the strongest backend within each representation, they improve over raw-text controls by an average of +0.137 AUPRC. LLM judges remain substantially weaker under the same prefix-warning protocol. We also derive an observability ceiling on score-based area under the precision-recall curve (AUPRC) that separates monitor error from failures lacking evidence in the observed prefix. For finite-state audit, post-hoc deterministic finite automaton (DFA) extraction remains compact on WebArena and τ^2-Bench (29 and 20 states) but expands to 151 and 187 states on SkillsBench and TerminalBench. Finally, first-alert diagnostics show that strong ranking does not imply deployment utility: WebArena ranks well yet fails to support low-false-alarm alerts, whereas τ^2-Bench and TerminalBench retain more actionable early alerts. Together, these results position PrefixGuard as a practical monitor-synthesis recipe with explicit diagnostics for when prefix warnings translate into actionable interventions.

ru.title: Ранее предупреждение об ошибках LLM-агентов через анализ префиксов
ru.desc: PrefixGuard — это фреймворк для синтеза мониторов, который позволяет отслеживать поведение LLM-агентов в реальном времени через анализ трасс выполнения. Система использует двухэтапный подход: сначала индуцирует типизированные адаптеры шагов из примеров трасс, затем обучает модель классификатора риска на префиксах действий агента. На бенчмарках WebArena, τ^2-Bench, SkillsBench и TerminalBench PrefixGuard достигает метрики AUPRC от 0.533 до 0.900, что значительно превосходит использование LLM-судей. Авторы показывают, что извлечённые автоматы имеют компактное представление и предлагают диагностику для определения, когда ранние предупреждения действительно полезны для вмешательства.

en.title: PrefixGuard: Real-Time Monitoring for LLM Agents
en.desc: The paper introduces PrefixGuard, a framework designed for monitoring large language model (LLM) agents during complex tasks. It utilizes trace analysis and prefix-based risk scoring to provide real-time alerts, improving the reliability of interventions. The framework includes an offline induction step to create typed-step adapters from raw traces, followed by supervised training to develop a risk scoring monitor. Results show that PrefixGuard significantly enhances performance across various benchmarks, offering actionable insights for effective monitoring of LLM agents.

zh.title: PrefixGuard：智能监控大语言模型的有效方案
zh.desc: PrefixGuard 是一种有效的在线监控大语言模型（LLM）代理的方法，通过追踪分析和基于前缀的风险评分来实现。它在多个基准任务中表现出色，并提供了可靠警报的诊断见解。该框架包括一个离线的 StepView 诱导步骤，随后进行监督监控训练，从而生成事件抽象和前缀风险评分器。研究表明，PrefixGuard 在监控性能上显著优于传统的文本控制方法，能够更好地支持低误报的警报。

papers.title: Gated QKAN-FWP: Scalable Quantum-inspired Sequence Learning
papers.abstract: Abstract Quantum-inspired fast-weight programming framework using single-qubit circuits achieves superior forecasting performance with reduced parameters compared to classical recurrent models while maintaining NISQ device compatibility.  AI-generated summary Fast Weight Programmers (FWPs) encode temporal dependencies through dynamically updated parameters rather than recurrent hidden states. Quantum FWPs (QFWPs) extend this idea with variational quantum circuits (VQCs), but existing implementations rely on multi-qubit architectures that are difficult to scale on noisy intermediate-scale quantum (NISQ) devices and expensive to simulate classically. We propose gated QKAN-FWP, a fast-weight framework that integrates FWP with Quantum-inspired Kolmogorov-Arnold Network (QKAN) using single-qubit data re-uploading circuits as learnable nonlinear activation, known as DatA Re-Uploading ActivatioN (DARUAN). We further introduce a scalar-gated fast-weight update rule that stabilizes parameter evolution, supported by a theoretical analysis of its adaptive memory kernel, geometric boundedness, and parallelizable gradient paths. We evaluate the framework across time-series benchmarks, MiniGrid reinforcement learning, and highlight real-world solar cycle forecasting as our main practical result. In the long-horizon setting with 528-month input window and 132-month forecast horizon, our 12.5k-parameter model achieves lower scaled Mean Square Error (MSE), peak amplitude error, and peak timing error than a suite of classical recurrent baselines with up to 13x more parameters, including Long Short-Term Memory (LSTM) networks (25.9k-89.1k parameters), WaveNet-LSTM (167k), Vanilla recurrent neural network (11.5k), and a Modified Echo State Network (132k). To validate NISQ compatibility, we further deploy the trained fast programmer on IonQ and IBM Quantum processors, recovering forecasting accuracy within 0.1% relative MSE of the noiseless simulator at 1024 shots. These results position gated QKAN-FWP as a scalable, parameter-efficient, and NISQ-compatible approach to quantum-inspired sequence modeling.
affiliations: National Center for High-Performance Computing, National Institutes of Applied Research

ru.title: Квантово-вдохновленные лёгкие сети для эффективного прогнозирования временных рядов
ru.desc: В работе предложена квантово-вдохновленная архитектура для прогнозирования временных рядов, основанная на быстрых весовых программистах (Fast Weight Programmers) с использованием однокубитных квантовых схем. Вместо традиционных скрытых состояний рекуррентных сетей модель динамически обновляет параметры через вариационные квантовые схемы с переиспользованием данных (DARUAN) в качестве нелинейной активации. Предложенный метод gated QKAN-FWP содержит в 13 раз меньше параметров, чем классические базовые модели (LSTM, WaveNet), но достигает лучшей точности прогноза на длинных временных горизонтах, включая реальное предсказание солнечного цикла. Модель успешно развернута на шумных квантовых процессорах IonQ и IBM Quantum, сохраняя точность и совместимость с современными NISQ-устройствами.

en.title: Efficient Quantum-Inspired Forecasting with Fewer Parameters
en.desc: This paper presents a new framework called gated QKAN-FWP, which combines fast-weight programming with quantum-inspired techniques to improve forecasting tasks. Unlike traditional recurrent models, this approach uses dynamically updated parameters to capture temporal dependencies, making it more efficient. The framework employs single-qubit circuits for its operations, which are easier to implement on current quantum devices. The results show that this model outperforms classical models in forecasting accuracy while using significantly fewer parameters, demonstrating its potential for practical applications in quantum computing.

zh.title: 量子启发的高效预测模型
zh.desc: 这篇论文提出了一种量子启发的快速权重编程框架，使用单量子比特电路来实现优越的预测性能。与传统的递归模型相比，该框架在参数数量上有所减少，同时保持了与噪声中间规模量子（NISQ）设备的兼容性。论文中介绍的门控量子Kolmogorov-Arnold网络（QKAN-FWP）通过动态更新参数来编码时间依赖性，避免了使用递归隐藏状态。实验结果表明，该模型在长时间序列预测中表现优异，且在多个基准测试中超越了多种经典递归模型。

papers.title: CASCADE: Case-Based Continual Adaptation for Large Language Models During Deployment
papers.abstract: Abstract Deployment-time learning enables large language model agents to adapt continuously during operation through episodic memory and contextual bandit optimization, improving performance across diverse tasks.  AI-generated summary Large language models (LLMs) have become a central foundation of modern artificial intelligence, yet their lifecycle remains constrained by a rigid separation between training and deployment, after which learning effectively ceases. This limitation contrasts with natural intelligence, which continually adapts through interaction with its environment. In this paper, we formalise deployment-time learning (DTL) as the third stage in the LLM lifecycle that enables LLM agents to improve from experience during deployment without modifying model parameters. We present CASCADE (CASe-based Continual Adaptation during DEployment), a general and principled framework that equips LLM agents with an explicit, evolving episodic memory. CASCADE formulates experience reuse as a contextual bandit problem, enabling principled exploration-exploitation trade-offs and establishing no-regret guarantees over long-term interactions. This design allows agents to accumulate, select, and refine task-relevant cases, transforming past experience into actionable knowledge. Across 16 diverse tasks spanning medical diagnosis, legal analysis, code generation, web search, tool use, and embodied interaction, CASCADE improves macro-averaged success rate by 20.9% over zero-shot prompting while consistently outperforming gradient-based and memory-based baselines. By reframing deployment as an adaptive learning process, this work establishes a foundation for continually improving AI systems.

ru.title: Обучение больших моделей прямо во время работы через память и оптимизацию опыта
ru.desc: Статья описывает развёртывание-ориентированное обучение (DTL) для больших языковых моделей, которое позволяет агентам улучшаться во время работы без изменения параметров модели. Авторы представляют фреймворк CASCADE, который использует эпизодическую память и решает задачу контекстного многорукого бандита для оптимизации выбора прошлых примеров. Система накапливает, отбирает и уточняет релевантные случаи из опыта, преобразуя их в практически полезное знание с гарантиями отсутствия сожаления. CASCADE продемонстрировал улучшение успеха на 20,9% на 16 различных задачах, включая медицинскую диагностику, анализ юридических документов и написание кода.

en.title: Empowering AI: Learning on the Fly with CASCADE
en.desc: This paper introduces deployment-time learning (DTL) as a new phase in the lifecycle of large language models (LLMs), allowing them to learn and adapt while in use. The authors present CASCADE, a framework that utilizes episodic memory to help LLM agents improve their performance based on past experiences without changing their underlying model parameters. By treating experience reuse as a contextual bandit problem, CASCADE enables effective exploration and exploitation strategies, ensuring that agents can learn from their interactions over time. The results show that CASCADE significantly enhances performance across various tasks, demonstrating the potential for continuous improvement in AI systems during deployment.

zh.title: 部署时学习：让AI不断进步的关键
zh.desc: 本文提出了一种新的学习方法，称为部署时学习（DTL），使大型语言模型（LLM）在运行过程中能够不断适应和改进。通过引入CASCADE框架，LLM代理可以利用显式的、不断发展的情景记忆来积累和选择与任务相关的经验。CASCADE将经验重用视为一个上下文赌博机问题，从而实现了探索与利用之间的平衡，并在长期交互中提供了无悔保证。通过在16个不同任务上的实验，CASCADE显著提高了成功率，展示了将部署视为适应性学习过程的潜力。

papers.title: CGM-JEPA: Learning Consistent Continuous Glucose Monitor Representations via Predictive Self-Supervised Pretraining
papers.abstract: Abstract A self-supervised pretraining framework for continuous glucose monitoring data achieves superior cross-modal and cross-cohort performance by predicting masked latent representations and incorporating cross-view distributional objectives.  AI-generated summary Continuous Glucose Monitoring (CGM) can detect early metabolic subphenotypes (insulin resistance, IR; β-cell dysfunction), but population-scale deployment faces two coupled problems. First, the same physiological state appears through multiple views (CGM time series, venous OGTT, Glucodensity summaries), so single-view representations fail to transfer when deployment shifts the modality or setting. Second, baselines perform inconsistently across these shifts. Both problems point to one remedy: representations that abstract away from any single view to capture higher-level temporal and distributional structure. We propose CGM-JEPA, a self-supervised pretraining framework which predicts masked latent representations rather than raw values, yielding abstraction that transfers across modalities. X-CGM-JEPA adds a masked Glucodensity cross-view objective for complementary distributional information. We pretrain on sim389k unlabeled CGM readings from 228 subjects and evaluate on two clinical cohorts (N=27 and N=17 public-release subsets) across three regimes (cohort generalization, venous-to-CGM transfer, home CGM) under 20-iteration times 2-fold cross-validation. X-CGM-JEPA ranks first or second on AUROC for both endpoints across all three regimes while no baseline does, exceeding the strongest baseline by up to +6.5 pp in cohort generalization and +3.6 pp in venous-to-CGM transfer (paired Wilcoxon, p<0.001). Under modality shift, it matches mean AUROC while redistributing toward weaker subgroups (ethnicity AUROC gap shrinks 25-54%); on sparse in-domain venous data, the distributional view lifts label-aware clustering (ARI +39%, NMI +40%). Code and weights: https://github.com/cruiseresearchgroup/CGM-JEPA

ru.title: Кросс-модальное предобучение для универсального понимания глюкозных данных
ru.desc: В статье предлагается CGM-JEPA — фреймворк самоконтролируемого предобучения для данных непрерывного мониторинга глюкозы, который предсказывает скрытые представления вместо сырых значений. Метод использует маскированное предсказание и кросс-модальные объективные функции для захвата высокоуровневой структуры временных рядов и распределений. Модель предварительно обучается на 389 тысячах немеченых измерений глюкозы от 228 пациентов и демонстрирует превосходную передачу знаний между разными модальностями (CGM временные ряды, венозные тесты, плотности) и различными когортами пациентов. X-CGM-JEPA показывает лучший результат по метрике AUROC во всех трех режимах оценки, превосходя базовые подходы на 3.6-6.5 процентных пункта и улучшая справедливость между этническими группами.

en.title: Transforming Glucose Monitoring with Self-Supervised Learning
en.desc: This paper presents CGM-JEPA, a self-supervised pretraining framework designed for continuous glucose monitoring (CGM) data. It addresses the challenges of transferring representations across different modalities and cohorts by predicting masked latent representations, which helps in capturing higher-level temporal and distributional structures. The framework incorporates a cross-view objective to enhance the model's performance on various clinical tasks. Results show that CGM-JEPA outperforms existing baselines in cohort generalization and modality transfer, demonstrating its effectiveness in improving predictive accuracy across diverse settings.

zh.title: 自监督学习提升血糖监测数据分析
zh.desc: 本论文提出了一种自监督预训练框架CGM-JEPA，用于连续血糖监测数据的分析。该框架通过预测被遮蔽的潜在表示，能够在不同的视角和人群中实现更好的性能。研究表明，CGM-JEPA在多个临床队列中表现优异，尤其是在群体泛化和血清转CGM的任务中。通过引入跨视角的分布目标，CGM-JEPA有效地捕捉了更高层次的时间和分布结构。
